# **II. Software Requirement Specification** {#ii.-software-requirement-specification}

## **1\. Overall Requirements** {#1.-overall-requirements}

### **1.1 Context Diagram** {#1.1-context-diagram}

The Omnichannel Sales System is a centralized software solution that streamlines sales, inventory management, and customer care across multiple channels, replacing older, disconnected manual processes. The context diagram below illustrates the external entities and system interfaces. The system is designed to connect internal staff with external e-commerce platforms to ensure a smooth flow of data. 

![][image2]

### **1.2 Main Business Processes** {#1.2-main-business-processes}

#### **1.2.1 Sales Order Processing**

![][image3]

#### **1.2.2 Return Order Processing**

![][image4]

### **1.3 User Requirements** {#1.3-user-requirements}

#### 1.3.1 Actors

| No. | Actor | Description |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **1** | **Administrator** *(System Admin)* | Rcesponsible for the technical setup and security of the system. Key tasks include creating new user accounts, granting initial roles, and configuring/testing API integrations (API Key, Secret Key, Webhook URL) to connect the system with external E-commerce platforms (Shopee, TikTok, etc.) |
| **2** | **Business Manager** | Plays the role of business operations, human resources management, and decision-making. This actor has the authority to update roles or deactivate staff accounts. Acts as the **Checker** to approve crucial warehouse operations (approving Master SKUs, inbound/outbound receipts, disposal requests, inventory transfers, and physical checks). Uses the Analytics module to view unified dashboards and monitor omnichannel revenue. |
| **3** | **Sales Staff** | Manages the B2C Hub omnichannel retail module. Responsible for configuring retail pricing, mapping SKUs, and publishing product information directly to E-commerce platforms via APIs. This actor handles the order lifecycle: manually reviewing risky orders, generating tracking numbers (waybills) in bulk, confirming "Ready to Ship" (RTS), and uploading video evidence to dispute RMA refund requests with platforms. |
| **4** | **Warehouse Staff** | Executes physical warehouse tasks. Responsibilities include editing product metadata, configuring warehouse zone locations of the products,  creating inbound/outbound/transfer receipts, picking and packing orders, using barcode scanners for physical inventory counting, and receiving return packages from carriers to place them into the "Dispute Zone" |
| **5** | **WMS System** *(Timer/Background Process)* | **(Automated system actor)** Executes background processes (Cronjobs) and continuously listens to Webhooks. Automatically releases soft-allocated inventory when orders are canceled, updates delivery journey statuses, and periodically syncs available inventory levels to E-commerce platforms to prevent overselling |
| **6** | **E-commerce Platform / Shipping API** *(External System)* | **(External system actor)** Represents third-party platforms such as Shopee, Lazada, TikTok, and Shipping Carriers. This actor provides order data, returns tracking numbers, receives inventory/product updates from the WMS, and sends Webhook notifications regarding order status changes or customer return requests |

#### 1.3.2 Use Cases (UC)

#### 

| ID | Use Case | Feature | Use Case Description |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| UC-SYS01 | Manage User Accounts | System & Security | System Admin creates new accounts and sets initial roles; Business Manager updates roles or deactivates staff accounts to ensure system security and proper access control. |
| UC-SYS02 | Verify Identity OTP | System & Security | Users authenticate their login attempts via an internally generated OTP sent via Gateway to securely access their dashboard. |
| UC-SYS03 | User Login | System & Security | As an internal user, I want to log into the system using my credentials so that I can access my designated dashboard. |
| UC-SYS04 | Forgot Password | System & Security | As an internal user who forgot my password, I want to reset my password securely so that I can regain access to my account. |
| UC-SYS05 | Change Password | System & Security | As a logged-in user, I want to change my current password to a new one from my account settings to maintain account security. |
| UC-SYS06 | Manage API Configurations | System & Security | System Admin configures API Keys, Secret Keys, and Webhook URLs to establish secure connections with external E-commerce platforms. |
| UC-SYS07 | Manage Own Profile | System & Security | Logged-in user views and updates their personal profile (full name, email, phone) from account settings. |
| UC-WH01 | Manage Master SKU Catalog | Warehouse Management | Business Manager manages the global product catalog by creating, editing, approving, or deleting Master SKUs to ensure data integrity. |
| UC-WH02 | Configure Warehouse SKU Storage | Warehouse Management | Warehouse Staff configures storage zones and min/max stock thresholds for approved SKUs in their assigned warehouse. |
| UC-WH03 | Configure Sales Pricing | Warehouse Management | Sales Staff configures retail and promotional prices for individual E-commerce channels. |
| UC-WH04 | Manage Inbound Receipt | Warehouse Management | Warehouse Staff creates receipts for received goods, and the Business Manager approves them to automatically increase physical and available inventory. |
| UC-WH05 | Manage Outbound Receipt | Warehouse Management | Warehouse Staff manages outbound list, creates issue notes for orders or disposal, and updates stock via approval workflows. |
| UC-WH06 | Pick & Pack Order | Warehouse Management | Warehouse Staff fulfills outbound notes by picking items, packing them, and generating shipping labels and delivery notes. |
| UC-WH07 | Transfer Inventory | Warehouse Management | Warehouse Staff creates and Business Manager approves inventory transfer requests to move physical stock between different zones or warehouse branches. |
| UC-WH08 | Create & Approve Physical Check | Warehouse Management | Warehouse Staff scans barcodes to count actual stock, and Business Manager approves discrepancies to automatically generate inbound/outbound adjustment receipts. |
| UC-WH09 | Process RMA Return Package | Warehouse Management | Warehouse Staff receives failed/returned deliveries and moves them directly to the Dispute Zone to prevent reselling damaged goods. |
| UC-WH10 | Configure Import Price | Warehouse Management | Warehouse Staff configures and updates the base import prices for Master SKUs to calculate COGS and inventory value. |
| UC-WH11 | Manage Warehouse Information & Zones | Warehouse Management | Warehouse Staff views assigned warehouse details and manages internal storage zones (creation, update, deletion). |
| UC-WH12 | Manage Inventory Ledger | Warehouse Management | Business Manager and Warehouse Staff view and process warehouse documents for auditing and tracking stock movements. |
| UC-B2C01 | Manage Channel SKU Mapping | B2C Retail Hub | Sales Staff links internal Master SKUs with external E-commerce SKUs (Many-to-Many) to enable accurate cross-channel data synchronization. |
| UC-B2C02 | Publish Product to Channels | B2C Retail Hub | Sales Staff configures platform-specific details (Categories, Brands) and pushes product information directly to platforms like Shopee/Lazada via API. |
| UC-B2C03 | Track & Filter Omnichannel Orders | B2C Retail Hub | Sales Staff views all incoming orders, filters delivery statuses, and checks cross-branch inventory in a centralized interface. |
| UC-B2C04 | Process Shipping Waybills | B2C Retail Hub | Sales Staff requests Tracking Numbers via E-commerce/Carrier APIs and bulk-prints shipping labels for packed orders. |
| UC-B2C05 | Manual Review & Select Warehouse | B2C Retail Hub | Sales Staff manually reviews risky orders, selects the dispatching warehouse branch, and triggers soft-allocation of inventory to prevent overselling. |
| UC-B2C06 | Sync RTS API (Ready to Ship) | B2C Retail Hub | Sales Staff confirms packed orders to trigger an API call notifying E-commerce platforms to dispatch logistics carriers for pickup. |
| UC-B2C07 | Update Order Delivery via Webhook | B2C Retail Hub | The background system automatically listens to carrier webhooks to update delivery journey statuses (e.g., Shipping, Delivered) in real-time. |
| UC-B2C08 | Dispute Platform RMA | B2C Retail Hub | Sales Staff reviews returned items in the Dispute Zone and uploads packing videos as evidence to E-commerce platforms to dispute unwarranted returns and claim compensation. |
| UC-B2C09 | Cancel B2C Order | B2C Retail Hub | The system (via webhook) or Sales Staff manually cancels orders to automatically release soft-allocated stock back to the available inventory pool. |
| UC-B2C10 | Sync Inventory to Channels API | B2C Retail Hub | A background cronjob continually calculates available stock minus safety buffer stock and pushes real-time inventory updates to E-commerce platforms to prevent overselling. |
| UC-RPT01 | View Unified Analytics Dashboards | Analytics | Business Manager views visualized data, pie charts, and top-selling product reports to analyze omnichannel revenue and make informed business decisions. |

| 16 | Document Details & Approval |  | X (Approve) |  |  |

| 18 | New Category |  | X |  |  |
| 19 | SKU Mapping |  |  | X |  |
| 20 | Mapping Form |  |  | X |  |

#### **1.4.3 Non-UI Functions**

| No. | Feature | System Function | Description |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| 1 | System & Security | **OTP Generation & Gateway API** | The system automatically generates an internal OTP (valid for 3 minutes) and triggers an external Email/SMS Gateway API to deliver it to the user for authentication. |
| 2 | Warehouse Management | **Auto Inventory Ledger Calculation** | A background service that automatically recalculates and updates qty\_physical and qty\_available, or releases qty\_allocated, whenever inbound/outbound receipts are completed. |
| 3 | Warehouse Management | **Auto Adjustment Tickets** | After a physical check discrepancy is approved, the system automatically generates an "Inbound Adjustment" or "Outbound Disposal" receipt to balance the physical inventory in the background. |
| 4 | Warehouse Management | **Low-Stock Auto Alert** | A background service that automatically triggers dashboard notification to the Warehouse Manager whenever a product's inventory falls below a predefined safety buffer threshold. |
| 5 | B2C Retail Hub | **Auto Order Mapping** | A background process that automatically maps every incoming order from E-commerce platforms to a specific registered customer profile and corresponding Master SKU in the local database. |
| 6 | B2C Retail Hub | **Inventory Soft-Allocation** | Upon receiving a new valid order, the system automatically moves the requested item quantity into a "Holding" status (increasing qty\_allocated and decreasing qty\_available) instead of deducting physical stock immediately. |
| 7 | B2C Retail Hub | **Order Delivery Webhook & Auto-Complete** | A background process listening to shipping carrier webhooks 24/7 to automatically update delivery journey statuses. **It also automatically sets the order status to "Completed" after a defined period (e.g., 3 days) if no RMA dispute is raised**. |
| 8 | B2C Retail Hub | **Cancel Order Auto-release** | Upon receiving an order cancellation webhook from E-commerce platforms, the system automatically releases the soft-allocated stock back to the available inventory pool. |
| 9 | B2C Retail Hub | **Auto Sync Inventory API (Cronjob)** | A background cronjob that periodically calculates available stock minus the safety buffer stock and automatically pushes real-time inventory updates to E-commerce platforms to prevent overselling |

### **1.5 Entity Relationship Diagram** {#1.5-entity-relationship-diagram}

![][image11]

![][image12]

Link DB: https://dbdiagram.io/d/ISP-Alt-6a1083beb62396d22c4c71cb

**Entities Description** 

|   No. | Entity | Description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | roles | List of user roles (admin, manager, sales\_staff, warehouse\_staff). Defines access permissions/privileges |
| 2 | users | Account information of employees using the system. Each employee has 1 role and can work in multiple warehouses |
| 3 | otp\_tokens | Stores 6-digit OTP codes used for two-factor authentication (2FA) login. OTP expires after 3 minutes. |
| 4 | branches | Information about the company's warehouses / branches. Each warehouse has a unique code (e.g., KHO-HCM-01). |
| 5 | user\_branch\_assignments | Employee-to-warehouse assignment table. 1 employee can work in multiple warehouses. |
| 6 | zones | Logical zones within the warehouse (normal, return, damaged, destroy). Partitions goods based on status. |
| 7 | categories | Product categories in a hierarchical tree (parent → child). |
| 8 | products | List of main products/goods. Each product has 1 unique internal SKU. Does not use variant SKUs. |
| 9 | product\_images | Product images stored as URLs on the Cloud (S3/Cloudinary). Does not store binary files in the DB. |
| 10 | channels | Integrated sales channels (Lazada, Shopee, TikTok Shop). Stores API configurations and Webhook Secrets. |
| 11 | channel\_products | Links products to sales channels. Stores specific selling prices and inventory pushed to each channel. |
| 12 | inventory | Actual inventory per product in each warehouse. Tracks 3 metrics: on\_hand, holding, available. |
| 13 | inventory\_ledger | Ledger recording all inventory movements/fluctuations. Append-only (no deletion). Used for auditing and reconciliation. |
| 14 | orders | Orders received from e-commerce platforms via Webhook. Stores the platform's order ID, processing status, and total amount. |
| 15 | order\_items | Details of products in each order. Stores a price snapshot at the time of ordering. |
| 16 | order\_shipping\_details | Shipping information: recipient name, address, shipping provider, tracking number. 1-to-1 relationship with orders. (Phone number removed — Lazada API does not provide it). |
| 17 | warehouse\_receipts | Official warehouse receipt notes (purchases, returned goods, goods from other warehouses). Must be approved to update inventory |
| 18 | receipt\_details | Product details in the warehouse receipt: quantity, unit import price. |
| 19 | warehouse\_issues | Warehouse issue notes (dispatching for delivery, disposal, warehouse transfer). Must be approved to deduct physical inventory. |
| 20 | issue\_details | Product details in the issue note: quantity, notes |
| 21 | rma\_requests | Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) / Return requests from customers. Stores Lazada return ID, return tracking number, and item location in the warehouse. |
| 22 | rma\_items | Details of each product in the return order. Stores Lazada's item-level return ID and refund amount. |
| 23 | qc\_inspections | Quality control (QC) inspection results for returned goods. Categorizes quantities into good condition (return to stock) and damaged (dispose/destroy). |
| 24 | physical\_inventories |  Periodic physical inventory counting tickets. Reconciles system inventory with actual physical count. |
| 25 | physical\_inventory\_details | Inventory counting details per product: system inventory, actual inventory, variance (negative=shortage, positive=overage/surplus). |
| 26 | stock\_transfers | Stock transfer notes between warehouses. Linked to the issue note (sending warehouse) and receipt note (receiving warehouse). |
| 27 | transfer\_details | Stock transfer product details: quantity sent and actual quantity received |
| 28 | webhook\_logs | Logs storing all Webhook events received from e-commerce platforms (raw JSON). Used for replaying in case of errors. |
| 29 | scrap\_records | Official scrap/disposal records for damaged goods. Generated from QC results, requires manager approval |

## **2\. Use Case Specifications** {#2.-use-case-specifications}

### **2.1 System & Security** {#2.1-system-&-security}

#### **2.1.1 UC-SYS01 | Manage User Accounts**

| Primary Actors | System Admin, Business Manager | Secondary Actors | None |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As an Admin or Manager, I want to manage internal user accounts and roles to ensure that the system is strictly decentralized and properly controlled. |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | The user has successfully logged into the system with System Admin or Business Manager privileges. |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | User account data is updated in the system. Any role assignments or account deactivations take effect immediately.  |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | **Manage User Accounts** 1\. Actor accesses the User Management screen. 2\. System displays the list of all internal users. 3\. **(If System Admin):** Actor clicks on create new user, inputs the employee's details, assigns an initial Role, and clicks submit. 4\. **(If Business Manager):** Actor views the personnel list, selects an existing user to update their Role, or clicks to Deactivate an employee's account. 5\. System validates the input information and saves the changes. 6\. System displays a success message. |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | **Step 5a \- Duplicate Information:** If the System Admin creates a new user but the inputted email or username already exists in the system database, the system rejects the creation and displays an error message. |  |  |

#### **2.1.2 UC-SYS02 | Verify Identity  OTP** 

| Primary Actors | All Internal Users (System Admin, Business Manager, Sales Staff, Warehouse Staff) | Secondary Actors | Email/SMS Gateway API |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As an internal user, I want to authenticate my login attempt via an internally generated OTP sent via a Gateway so that my account and the system data are securely protected against unauthorized access. |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | The user has entered the correct Username and Password on the User Login screen. |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The system successfully verifies the user's identity, allowing the calling Use Case to proceed to its next step.  |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | **Verify OTP** 1\. **System** internally generates a 6-digit OTP which is valid for 3 minutes. 2\. **System** calls the external Email/SMS Gateway API to deliver the OTP to the user's registered email or phone number. 3\. **System** displays the OTP Verification modal/screen. 4\. **User** inputs the received OTP and clicks "Verify". 5\. **System** validates the OTP successfully and returns a success status to the main process. |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | Step 5a \- Incorrect or Expired OTP: If the user inputs an invalid or expired OTP, the system displays an error message prompting them to try again or request a new OTP. |  |  |

#### 2.1.2 UC-SYS03 |User Login 

#### 

| Primary Actors | All Internal Users (System Admin, Business Manager, Sales Staff, Warehouse Staff)  | Secondary Actors | None |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As an internal user, I want to log into the system using my credentials so that I can access my designated dashboard.  |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | The user is at the User Login screen.  |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | A secure working session is successfully established, and the user is granted access to the system dashboard based on their role.  |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | **Login Flow** 1\. **User** enters valid credentials (Username/Password) and clicks "Login". 2\. **System** validates the password and **includes UC-SYS02 (Verify Identity via OTP)**. 3\. Upon successful OTP verification from UC-SYS02, **System** grants the access token. 4\. **System** redirects the user to their designated Dashboard based on their Role. |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | **Step 1a \- Invalid Credentials:** If the username or password is incorrect, the system rejects the login attempt, blocks the OTP generation, and displays an error message. |  |  |

#### 2.1.2 UC-SYS04 | Forgot Password 

#### 

| Primary Actors | All Internal Users (System Admin, Business Manager, Sales Staff, Warehouse Staff)  | Secondary Actors | None |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As an internal user who forgot my password, I want to reset my password securely so that I can regain access to my account.  |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | The user is at the "Forgot Password" screen.  |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The user's password is updated in the system database, and they can use the new password to log in.  |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | Forgot Password Flow 1\. **User** enters their registered Email or Phone number and clicks "Submit". 2\. **System** checks the database and includes UC-SYS02 (Verify Identity via OTP). 3\. Upon successful OTP verification from UC-SYS02, **System** displays the "Reset Password" screen. 4\. **User** enters a new password, confirms it, and clicks "Save". 5\. **System** updates the new password in the database and displays a success message. |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | **Step 1a \- Email/Phone Not Found:** If the inputted email or phone number does not exist in the system database, the system displays an error message: "Account not found." **Step 4a \- Password Mismatch:** If the new password and confirmed password fields do not match, the system prevents saving, highlights the fields, and prompts the user to re-type. |  |  |

#### 2.1.2 UC-SYS05 | Change Password 

#### 

| Primary Actors | All Internal Users (System Admin, Business Manager, Sales Staff, Warehouse Staff)  | Secondary Actors | None |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a logged-in user, I want to change my current password to a new one from my account settings to maintain account security.  |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | The user has successfully logged into the system and is on the Account Settings screen.  |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The user's password is secure and updated. All other active sessions of this user are terminated for security reasons.  |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | **Change Password Flow** 1\. **User** enters their current password, inputs a new password, confirms it, and clicks "Change Password". 2\. **System** validates that the current password is correct and includes UC-SYS02 (Verify Identity via OTP). 3\. Upon successful OTP verification from UC-SYS02, System saves the new password to the database. 4\. **System** terminates all other active sessions for security and displays a "Password Changed Successfully" message. |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | **Step 1a \- Incorrect Current Password:** If the current password entered by the user is incorrect, the system blocks the OTP request and displays an error message. **Step 1b \- Password Mismatch:** If the new password and confirmed password do not match, the system displays an error and prompts the user to check their input. |  |  |

#### 2.1.3 UC-SYS06 | Manage API Configurations

#### 

| Primary Actors | System Admin | Secondary Actors | None |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a System Admin, I want to manage the API configuration parameters of integrated third-party services to ensure that the system's connected services always operate stably, accurately, and securely.  |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | The user has successfully logged into the system with **System Admin** privileges.  |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | New or updated API configuration parameters are saved to the database and applied immediately to the system's API calling modules. . |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | **Manage API Configurations** 1\. **Actor** accesses the System Configuration menu and selects "Manage API Configurations". 2\. **System** displays a list of current API configurations (e.g., SMS Gateway, Email Service, Payment Gateway) along with their status (Active / Inactive). 3\. **Actor** selects a specific API to edit or clicks the "Add New API Configuration" button. 4\. **Actor** enters or modifies the technical parameters of the API, including: \* *API Name / Provider* \* *Base URL / Endpoint* \* *APP ID / PARTNER ID* \* *Secret Key / Access Token* 5\. **Actor** clicks the "Test Connection" button. 6\. **System** sends a test request to the third-party service, receives a successful response (HTTP Status 200 OK), and displays a "Connection Successful" message. 7\. **Actor** clicks "Save". 8\. **System** encrypts sensitive information (such as the Secret Key), saves it to the database, and displays a success message.  |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | **Step 6a \- Connection Test Failed:** If the system sends the test request but receives an error (e.g., Invalid API Key, incorrect URL, or Timeout), the system retains the inputted data on the screen, displays a "Connection Failed" message along with the detailed error code for the Admin to troubleshoot. **Step 7a \- Missing Required Fields:** If the Admin leaves required fields blank (such as Endpoint or API Key) and clicks Save, the system blocks the action, highlights the missing fields in red, and displays a message prompting the user to fill in all required information.  |  |  |

#### 

#### 2.1.4 UC-SYS07 | Manage Own Profile

| Primary Actors | All Internal Users (System Admin, Business Manager, Sales Staff, Warehouse Staff)  | Secondary Actors | None |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a logged-in user, I want to view and update their own profile information (full name, email, phone) and change their account password from the account-settings screen. Account deletion is not supported for the staff themselves.  |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | The user has successfully logged into the system and is on the Account Settings screen.  |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The user's profile fields and/or password hash are updated in the database; the session is synchronized with the new values.  |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | 1\. The user opens the Account Settings screen. 2\. The system loads the current profile from the database. 3\. Warehouse Staff edits full name / email / phone and clicks Save changes. 4\. System validates (non-empty name, valid email format), updates the database and the session, and shows a success message.  |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** |  |  |  |

### 

### **2.2 Warehouse Management**

#### **2.2.1 UC-WH01 | Manage Master SKU Catalog**

#### 

| Primary Actors | Business Manager | Secondary Actors | None |
| ----- | :---- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a Business Manager, I want to manage the global product catalog by creating new Master SKUs, editing core product metadata, and suspending or deleting SKUs, ensuring a single, clean source of product truth for multi-channel synchronization. |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | The Business Manager is authenticated in the system with the MANAGER role. Required Product Categories have been defined. |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The Master SKU record (metadata, unit, dimensions, weight) and its lifecycle status are persisted in the global database. |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | Business Manager navigates to the Master SKU Catalog management page. The system loads and displays the list of all Master SKUs in the global registry (showing SKU code, name, category, status, and creation audit info). Business Manager clicks on the Create Master SKU button. The system opens the creation form requesting core metadata: SKU Code, Product Name, Category, Barcode, Unit, Weight (kg), and Dimensions/Attributes.  Business Manager fills in the required details and clicks Save. The system validates the input (e.g., checks if the SKU Code is unique and all required fields are filled). The system registers the new SKU in a transaction and displays a success message. |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** |  |  |  |

#### **2.2.2 UC-WH02 | Configure Warehouse SKU Storage**

| Primary Actors | Warehouse Staff | Secondary Actors | None |
| ----- | :---- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a Warehouse Staff member, I want to configure the localized storage zones, minimum stock levels, and maximum stock levels for approved (Active) Master SKUs in the specific warehouse I manage, ensuring optimal replenishment and stock allocation. |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | The Warehouse Staff is authenticated with the WAREHOUSE\_STAFF role. The Warehouse Staff is assigned to an active warehouse. At least one Master SKU is set to Approved (Active) in the global catalog. |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The SKU’s storage zone mapping, minimum threshold, and maximum threshold are persisted for the assigned warehouse. No global SKU records are created or deleted by the Warehouse Staff. |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | Warehouse Staff opens the Warehouse Master SKU screen. The system loads the list of active Master SKUs, along with their localized statistics for the assigned warehouse (on-hand stock, assigned storage zone, min/max thresholds, and warning flags), plus the staff's own warehouse id. Warehouse Staff filters by category and/or searches by SKU code or name to find the desired SKU, export to CSV. Warehouse Staff clicks Configure Storage on the SKU. The system opens the storage configuration modal, showing the assigned warehouse, a dropdown of available storage zones within that warehouse, and inputs for min/max thresholds. Warehouse Staff selects the target storage zone, enters the minimum and maximum stock limits, and clicks Save. The system validates that the values are valid numbers, non-negative, and that the minimum limit is less than or equal to the maximum limit. The system updates the zone mapping and thresholds for the SKU in the database, and displays a success notification with the refreshed list. |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows**  | A1 \- Export CSV: Staff exports the (filtered) list to a client-side generated UTF-8 (BOM) CSV file. A2 \- View Detail: Staff opens a read-only modal showing global SKU metadata and location details. A3 \- Validation Error: Threshold inputs are invalid (non-numeric, negative, or min \> max); system shows error and does not save. |  |  |

#### **2.2.3 UC-WH03 | Configure Sales Pricing**

#### 

| Primary Actors | Sales Staff | Secondary Actors | None |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a staff member, I want to configure the retail selling prices for individual e-commerce channels so that the system can calculate profit margins accurately and synchronize pricing to platforms. |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | Sales Staff user must be logged in with appropriate permissions At least one Master SKU must exist in the system with "Active" status Multiple sales channels must be configured in the system (Shopee, TikTok, Lazada, Website) SKU products must be mapped to at least one sales channel System must have pricing data accessible for the currently selected channel |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | Pricing changes are saved to the database for the selected channel All price modifications are persisted and reflected in the pricing table Stats dashboard updates to show current active SKU count and lock status Inactive (locked) SKUs cannot be modified System displays confirmation or success message after save operation |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | **Configure Retail Prices** 1\. Navigate to "Cấu hình giá bán" (Configure Pricing) tab in the Sales Channel Products interface  2\. View dashboard stats showing: SKU đang Active (Active SKUs), Kênh đang quản lý (Channels Managed), SKU không cho sửa (Inactive/Locked SKUs) 3\. Select a sales channel by clicking one of the channel label (Shopee, TikTok, Lazada, Website)  4\. View pricing table displaying: Master SKU, Product Name, Status, Import Price (Giá nhập), Retail Price, Promo Price  5\. Locate desired product in table and enter new Retail Price in the corresponding input field 6\. Enter new Promotional Price in the Promo Price input field  7\. Click "Lưu" (Save) button on the pricing row to save individual price changes   8\. Repeat steps 5-7 for additional products as needed  9\. Display save status confirmation message   |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | Error 1: Attempt to Edit Inactive/Locked SKU \- Condition: User clicks on disabled price input field \- Response: Field remains disabled; cursor shows "not-allowed" icon \- User Action: Select active SKU to modify pricing Error 2: Channel Data Load Failed \- Condition: Selected channel has no pricing data \- Response: Empty table displayed; user can retry by switching channel \- User Action: Refresh page or select different channel  |  |  |

#### **2.2.4 UC-WH04 | Manage Inbound Receipt**

#### 

| Primary Actors | Warehouse Staff, Business Manager | Secondary Actors | None |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As warehouse personnel, we want to create and approve inbound goods receipts so that the physical inventory ledger is accurately securely updated based on the Maker-Checker principle. |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | Physical goods have arrived from the supplier, and the actor is logged in with the corresponding role. |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The system automatically increases both qty\_physical and qty\_available in the database. The receipt is permanently recorded. |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | **Process Inbound Receipt** 1\. Warehouse Staff physically counts the received items and accesses the Inbound List screen. 2\. Staff inputs the SKU, actual quantity, and the associated Purchase Order (PO) ID to create a Draft Inbound Receipt. 3\. Staff clicks "Complete Inbound" to submit the receipt. The system locks the receipt, changing its status to Read-only. 4\. Business Manager accesses the Inventory Transactions screen to review the submitted receipt. 5\. Business Manager verifies the details and clicks "Approve". 6\. System executes the background process to automatically add the received quantity to both qty\_physical and qty\_available in the Inventory Ledger. 7\. System displays a success message. |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | **Step 2a \- Inbound Validation Error (BR-07):** If the Warehouse Staff attempts to submit the receipt but leaves the Purchase Order ID blank or inputs an invalid PO ID, the system blocks the creation and displays an error message to ensure accountability. **Step 5a \- Manager Rejects Receipt:** If the Business Manager finds a discrepancy during the review, they click "Reject" and add a mandatory comment. The receipt is unlocked and reverted to "Draft" status for the Warehouse Staff to correct. |  |  |

#### **2.2.5 UC-WH05 | Create Outbound Note**

#### 

| Primary Actors | Warehouse Staff | Secondary Actors | Business Manager, Sales Staff |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a Warehouse Staff, I want to create outbound notes from fulfillment requests or sales orders for Business Manager approval, creates disposal (scrap) notes for damaged goods, so that customer orders are dispatched accurately and on time while damaged stock and approvals stay under control.  |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | The actor is authenticated as WAREHOUSE\_STAFF and assigned to a warehouse. For an outbound note: at least one fulfillment request / order is available.  For a disposal note: at least one damaged product exists.  |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | An outbound note is saved to outbound\_orders with status \= PENDING, scoped to the session warehouse. No stock is deducted; deduction is deferred to Business Manager approval. A disposal (scrap) note is saved to warehouse\_issues and warehouse\_issue\_details for the session warehouse. No stock is deducted at this step.  |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | **Flow 1 — Create outbound note from fulfillment request** 1\. Warehouse Staff opens the Outbound screen (/warehouse/outbound); the system loads outbound orders and pending fulfillment requests scoped to the session warehouse. 2\. Warehouse Staff selects a fulfillment request and submits (POST action=create, orderId, notes). 3\. The system reads warehouseId from the session (never from the form) and calls validateForCreate(orderId, warehouseId). 4\. On validation pass, the system generates an outbound code (SOUT-YYYYMMDD-XXX), inserts a record into outbound\_orders with status \= PENDING, and returns the new outbound\_id. 5\. The system redirects to /warehouse/outbound and shows flash message “Tạo phiếu xuất kho thành công. Chờ Business Manager duyệt.”   **Flow 2 — Create disposal (scrap) note** 1\. Warehouse Staff opens the disposal modal and submits (POST action=disposal, sku, qty, reason). 2\. The system reads warehouseId from the session and parses qty as BigDecimal (exact decimal, no floating-point rounding). 3\. The system calls createDisposal(sku, qty, reason, warehouseId, userId): it looks up the product by SKU code, then calls createScrapIssue() which inserts into warehouse\_issues (SCRAP, DRAFT) and warehouse\_issue\_details in a single transaction. 4\. No stock deduction occurs at this step. 5\. The system redirects to /warehouse/outbound and shows flash message “Đã lưu phiếu xuất huỷ. Tồn kho khả dụng chưa bị trừ.”  |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | 6A \- Manager rejects: the Business Manager clicks 'Reject' with a reason, no stock moved. \*A \- Invalid input: missing order, quantity \<= 0, missing reason/evidence, or unknown SKU \-\> error, nothing persisted.  |  |  |

#### **2.2.6 UC-WH06 | Pick & Pack Order**

#### 

| Primary Actors | Warehouse Staff | Secondary Actors | None |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a Warehouse Staff, I want to print picking lists, pick items from the correct zones, pack them accurately and generate delivery notes so that customer orders are successfully prepared for shipping. |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | The actor is authenticated as WAREHOUSE\_STAFF. An outbound request (Order ID) has been initialized and assigned to the warehouse. |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The order status advances PENDING \-\> PICKING \-\> PACKED \-\> ISSUED. |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | 1\. Warehouse Staff opens Outbound and locates an order with status 'Pending preparation'. 2\. Warehouse Staff clicks Start Picking. 3\. The system sets status PICKING, creates a picking\_sheets record, assigns the staff as picker, and displays the picking sheet (SKU, quantity, shelf location). 4\. Warehouse Staff collects the items and ticks each picked line; the system persists picked\_qty for each item (AJAX). 5\. Warehouse Staff clicks Confirm Packing. 6\. The system sets status PACKED, marks all items picked, completes the picking sheet, and auto-generates the shipping label (carrier and tracking pre-filled). 7\. Warehouse Staff confirms / edits the shipping label, then clicks Dispatch. 8\. The system sets status ISSUED, auto-generates the delivery note, and for omnichannel orders auto-approves the issue and deducts stock through the inventory ledger.  |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | 3a — Validation fail (outbound): missing orderId or invalid warehouseId → system shows flash error, persists nothing. 3b — Product not found (disposal): SKU does not exist in the catalog → failure("Không tìm thấy sản phẩm với SKU: ..."), nothing persisted. 3c — Invalid quantity (disposal): qty ≤ 0 or non-numeric → validation error, nothing persisted. \*a — warehouseId always from session: form fields for warehouseId are ignored; staff cannot create documents for another warehouse.   Note: Viewing the outbound list, pick & pack, status updates, and cancellation are covered in UC-WH05. Business Manager approval and stock deduction are covered in UC-WH11.  |  |  |

#### **2.2.7 UC-WH07 | Transfer Inventory**

#### 

| Primary Actors | Warehouse Staff, Business Manager | Secondary Actors | None |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As warehouse personnel, we want to create and approve inventory transfer requests to move physical stock between different zones or warehouse branches to optimize storage space and operational flow. |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | Sufficient physical stock must exist in the source location/zone, and the actors are logged in with their respective roles. |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The physical location of the transferred stock is updated in the database. The total company inventory quantity remains unchanged. |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | **Process Inventory Transfer** 1\. Warehouse Staff accesses the Stock Transfer screen. 2\. Staff clicks to create a "New Stock Transfer" and inputs the Source Zone (e.g., General Zone), Destination Zone (e.g., Defective Zone), SKU, and transfer Quantity. 3\. Staff submits the request. The system saves it with a "Pending Approval" status. 4\. Business Manager accesses the All Inventory Transactions screen to review the pending transfer. 5\. Business Manager verifies the reason/details and clicks "Approve". 6\. System executes the transfer, deducting stock from the Source Zone and adding it to the Destination Zone. 7\. System generates a Delivery Note for the internal transfer if items are moved between different physical warehouse branches. |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | **Step 5a \- Reject Transfer:** If the Business Manager finds the transfer unnecessary or incorrect, they click "Reject". The request is canceled, and the stock remains in its original zone. **Step 6a \- Insufficient Stock during Approval:** If the source stock falls below the requested transfer amount while pending (e.g., an urgent sales order deducted it first), the system blocks the approval process and alerts the Manager. |  |  |

#### **2.2.8 UC-WH08 | Create & Approve Physical Check**

#### 

| Primary Actors | Warehouse Staff, Business Manager | Secondary Actors | None |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As warehouse personnel, we want to perform periodic physical stocktakes and approve any discrepancies so that the system's ledger perfectly matches the actual physical inventory in the warehouse. |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | A stocktake session is initiated by management for specific storage zones or product categories. |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The system's qty\_physical matches the actual counted quantity. Auto Adjustment Tickets are generated to balance the ledger. |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | **Process Physical Inventory Check** 1\. Warehouse Staff accesses the Stocktake screen and opens a new Stocktake Sheet. 2\. Staff uses a handheld barcode scanner to scan physical item barcodes and inputs the actual physical count for each SKU. 3\. System automatically calculates and displays the discrepancy (Variance) between the physical count and the current system quantity. 4\. Staff submits the Stocktake Sheet. 5\. Business Manager reviews the discrepancies and clicks "Approve". 6\. System automatically generates an "Inventory Adjustment Note" ("Inbound Adjustment" if surplus, or "Outbound Disposal" if missing) in the background to synchronize qty\_physical with the actual count. 7\. System displays a success message. |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | **Step 5a \- Recount Required:** If the Business Manager spots a suspicious or massive discrepancy, they click "Reject" and add a comment requiring the Warehouse Staff to recount the specific Zone. The Stocktake Sheet reverts to "Draft" status for correction. |  |  |

#### **2.2.9 UC-WH09 | Process RMA Return Package**

#### 

| Primary Actors | Warehouse Staff | Secondary Actors | None |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a warehouse staff member, I want to scan and receive returned or failed delivery packages into a quarantine zone (Dispute Zone) without adding them back to the available stock, ensuring that potentially damaged goods are not resold. |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | A returned package (RMA or failed delivery) is physically delivered back to the warehouse by the shipping carrier. |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The returned package is recorded and isolated in the Dispute Zone. The system explicitly does NOT add the item back to the available inventory (qty\_available) to avoid data discrepancy. |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | **Receive Return Package** 1\. Warehouse Staff receives the return package and accesses the RMA Processing screen. 2\. Staff uses a barcode scanner to scan the return waybill barcode. 3\. System identifies the corresponding Order ID. 4\. Staff selects "Receive to Dispute Zone" and clicks submit. 5\. System logs the physical receipt of the package without modifying the available stock. 6\. Staff physically places the items into the Dispute Zone (Quarantine Zone), waiting for Sales QA. |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | **Step 3a \- Invalid Waybill Barcode:** If the scanned barcode does not match any existing order or waybill in the system, the system displays an error message (MSG03) prompting the staff for manual verification. |  |  |

#### **2.2.10 UC-WH10 | Configure Import Price**

#### 

| Primary Actors | Warehouse Staff | Secondary Actors | None |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a warehouse staff member, I want to configure and update the base import prices for products (Master SKUs) so that the system can accurately calculate the total inventory valuation and Cost of Goods Sold (COGS). |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | \- The warehouse staff is successfully logged into the system. \- Master SKUs are already created and exist in the system's database. |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | \- The new import price is saved to the system database. \- The system automatically recalculates the total equivalent inventory value (COGS) based on the updated price and available stock. |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | **Configure Import Prices** 1\. Warehouse Staff accesses the "Import Price Configuration" tab within the Inbound Management section. 2\. The system displays a list of all active Master SKUs, showing their current available stock and existing import prices. 3\. The system calculates and displays the overall summary, including total active SKUs, average import price, and total inventory valuation. 4\. Warehouse Staff enters a new import price value into the input field for one or multiple SKUs. 5\. Warehouse Staff clicks the "Save" button for a specific SKU or the "Save All" button to update multiple SKUs at once. 6\. The system validates the input to ensure it is a valid positive number. 7\. The system updates the price in the database and recalculates the COGS for the affected items on the screen. 8\. The system displays a success message confirming the update.  |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | A1. Invalid Price Input 1\. In step 6, if the Warehouse Staff enters a negative number or invalid characters, the system rejects the input. 2\. The system displays an error message requesting a valid positive amount and prevents saving. |  |  |

#### **2.2.11 UC-WH11 | Manage Warehouse Information & Zones**

#### 

| Primary Actors | Warehouse Staff | Secondary Actors | None |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a Warehouse Staff, I want to view the details of the warehouse I am assigned to and manage its storage zones, so that the warehouse layout stays accurate and goods are stored in the correct zones.  |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | The actor is authenticated as WAREHOUSE\_STAFF; warehouse\_id is read from the session. The assigned warehouse exists.  |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | A zone is created / updated / deleted in the zones table, scoped to the staff's own warehouse (every write is filtered by warehouse\_id). System default zones cannot be deleted; a delete that is blocked by references falls back to deactivation (active \= 0).  |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | **Flow: View info & manage zones** 1\. Warehouse Staff opens /warehouse/information; the system loads the staff's warehouse and its zones via findById(warehouseId from session). 2\. The screen shows warehouse master data (read-only) and the zones table. 3\. Create zone: Warehouse Staff submits '+ Thêm khu' (code, name, type, description); the system inserts a zone for the session warehouse (unique code per warehouse). 4\. Update zone: Warehouse Staff edits a zone (name, type, description); the system updates it, scoped by zone\_id AND warehouse\_id. 5\. Delete zone: Warehouse Staff deletes a non-default zone; the system deletes it (or deactivates it if it is still referenced). 6\. After each write the system redirects back to /warehouse/information (with the application context path) and shows a flash success / error message.  |  |  |
| Alternative Sequences/Flows | 3a \- Duplicate / invalid: empty code or name, invalid zone type, or a duplicate code in the warehouse \-\> the system shows an error and persists nothing. 5a \- Default zone: deleting a system default zone is rejected ('Không thể xóa khu mặc định của hệ thống.'). \*a \- Cross-warehouse safety: a zone that does not belong to the session warehouse is never affected (warehouse\_id scope).  |  |  |

| Primary Actors | Business Manager | Secondary Actors | None |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a Business Manager, I want to manage the system's warehouse catalog (Create new warehouses, Update warehouse profiles and storage zones, and Toggle warehouse active status). |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | User is authenticated with the MANAGER role. |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | Database records in warehouses and zones tables are updated. |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | Access Screen: Manager opens /business/warehouses; the system loads the shell and forwards to warehouses.jsp Load Data Create Warehouse: Manager fills in details/default zones and clicks Save. Update Warehouse: Manager edits warehouse or zone details and clicks Save.  Toggle Status: Manager toggles active switch.  |  |  |
| Alternative Sequences/Flows | 3A/4A \- Duplicate Warehouse Code: If database constraint fails on duplicate code, DAO rolls back the transaction. Servlet returns {"success": false, "message": "..."} and UI displays a failure toast. 4B \- Referential Integrity Protection: During update, if a deleted zone is still referenced by inventory, the SQL delete fails. DAO catches this foreign key error and soft-deactivates the zone (active \= 0\) instead to maintain historical records.  |  |  |

#### **2.2.12 UC-WH12 | Manage Inventory Ledger**

#### 

| Primary Actors | Business Manager, Warehouse Staff | Secondary Actors | None |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a Warehouse Staff I want to view the warehouse documents of MY warehouse (inbound, outbound, transfer, stocktake, return, disposal) so that stock movements are traceable.  As a Business Manager I view all warehouses and approve / reject pending documents.  |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | The actor is logged into the internal system with their respective role.  |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | Documents are viewed; (Business Manager only) approved \- stock movements applied to inventory and an entry written to inventory\_ledger \- or rejected \- status updated with a reason.  |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | 1\. The actor opens the Stock Ledger: Business Manager \-\> /business/ledger; Warehouse Staff \-\> /warehouse/documents. 2\. For Warehouse Staff the system calls findAllDocuments(warehouseId) and aggregates the 6 sources filtered by warehouse\_id (inbound\_orders, outbound\_orders, stock\_transfers, physical\_inventories, return\_orders, warehouse\_issues/SCRAP). Stock transfers are included when the warehouse is the source OR the destination (from\_warehouse\_id \= ? OR to\_warehouse\_id \= ?). The Business Manager calls findAllDocuments() (all warehouses) plus the global inventory\_ledger. 3\. The actor filters by document type via tabs (All, Inbound, Outbound, Transfer, Stocktake, RMA, Disposal) and/or searches by document code. 4\. The actor opens a document to view its line items (loaded on demand via AJAX) and can print PDF (client-side). 5\. Business Manager only: Approve applies the stock movement and writes an inventory\_ledger entry; Reject requires a reason and sets the status to CANCELLED (or DRAFT for stocktakes). Warehouse Staff have view / search / print only. 6\. On approval the status transitions: Inbound PENDING \-\> RECEIVED; Outbound PENDING \-\> SHIPPED; Transfer IN\_TRANSIT \-\> RECEIVED; Stocktake IN\_PROGRESS \-\> APPROVED. 7\. The list refreshes; the document shows its new status and a matching inventory\_ledger entry exists.  |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | 5A \- Reject without reason: the system prompts for a reason. 5B \- Already processed: approving a document in a final state is a no-op (no double-processing). 5C \- Warehouse Staff: /warehouse/documents shows no Approve/Reject buttons; only the Business Manager (via /business/ledger) can approve/reject.  |  |  |

### **2.3 B2C Retail Hub**

#### **2.3.1 UC-B2C01 | Manage Channel SKU Mapping**

| Primary Actors | Sales Staff | Secondary Actors | External E-commerce API (Shopee, Lazada, TikTok) |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a Sales Staff, I want to map internal Master SKUs to external E-commerce SKUs to establish a Many-to-Many relationship, ensuring accurate cross-channel data synchronization for inventory and orders. |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | Master SKUs are already created and active in the local database, and the API connections to E-commerce platforms are authorized and valid. |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The Many-to-Many mapping records are successfully saved in the database, serving as the foundation for order routing and inventory sync. |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | **Map Channel SKUs** 1\. Sales Staff accesses the SKU Mapping (Mapping Form) screen. 2\. System calls the external API to fetch and display the list of products from connected E-commerce platforms. 3\. Sales Staff selects an unmapped external E-commerce SKU. 4\. Sales Staff searches and selects one or multiple corresponding internal Master SKUs to create a mapping link (Many-to-Many). 5\. Staff clicks "Save Mapping". 6\. System validates and saves the mapping record in the database. 7\. System displays a success message. |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | **Step 2a \- API Disconnection:** If the API token is expired or the connection fails, the system cannot fetch external products and displays a warning banner (MSG06) asking the Admin to re-authorize the connection. |  |  |

#### **2.3.2 UC-B2C02 | Publish Product to Channels**

| Primary Actors | Sales Staff | Secondary Actors | External E-commerce API (Shopee, Lazada, TikTok) |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a Sales Staff, I want to configure platform-specific details and push internal Master SKUs directly to E-commerce platforms via API so that I don't have to manually re-enter product data on each channel. |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | The selected Master SKU must be in "Active" status, and its pricing configurations must be completed (UC-WH02). The API connection to the target platform must be valid. |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The product is successfully published on the external E-commerce platform, and a Many-to-Many mapping record is automatically generated in the local database. |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | **Publish Product via API** 1\. Sales Staff accesses the Product Configuration screen (under Channel Management). 2\. Staff selects an active Master SKU and chooses the target E-commerce platform (e.g., Shopee, Lazada). 3\. Staff inputs platform-specific required parameters (e.g., Platform Category, Brand, Logistics dimensions). 4\. Staff clicks the "Publish" button. 5\. System calls the external E-commerce API to push the product information payload. 6\. External API returns a "200 OK" success response with the newly created E-commerce SKU ID. 7\. System automatically creates a Many-to-Many mapping record linking the Master SKU to the E-commerce SKU. 8\. System displays a success toast message. |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | **Step 6a \- API Validation Error:** If the inputted data violates the platform's specific rules (e.g., missing mandatory brand, invalid category mapping), the external API rejects the request. The system catches the error and displays the exact validation message from the platform so the staff can correct the data and retry. |  |  |

#### **2.3.3 UC-B2C03 | Track & Filter Omnichannel Orders**

| Primary Actors | Sales Staff | Secondary Actors | None |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a sales staff, I want to view, track, and filter all incoming multi-channel orders from a centralized interface so I can easily monitor delivery status. |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | Sales Staff user must be logged in with appropriate permissions and accessed the Order List screen The system database contains existing order records (including data aggregated from integrated channels like Shopee, Lazada, TikTok, Website)  |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The filtered or searched dataset is presented accurately based on real-time data states. No modification to the core order states occurs during this read-only tracking process. |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | The actor navigates to the "Tất cả đơn hàng" (All Orders) section from the sidebar menu. The system retrieves and displays the comprehensive list of omnichannel orders with default status tabs and sorting. The actor views summary metrics for each order state (e.g., Chờ xác nhận, Chờ ĐVVC lấy hàng, Đang giao, Đã giao, Hoàn thành, Hoàn hàng). The actor applies search criteria or filters (by Channel, Status, Product, Shipping Carrier, or keywords like Order ID/Tracking ID/Customer Name/SKU). The system validates the filters and dynamically refreshes the data grid to display matching results. The actor reviews specific order details directly from the grid (Order ID, Channel, Customer Name & Phone, Quantity, Total Amount, Order Status, Fulfillment Warehouse).  |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | Alternative Flow A: Filtering by Quick-Tabs At step 4 of the Basic Flow, the actor clicks on a specific quick-status tab (e.g., "Chờ xác nhận"). The system filters the data grid to display only orders matching that specific lifecycle state. The flow returns to step 6 of the Basic Flow. Alternative Flow B: Deep-Dive Order Inspection At step 6 of the Basic Flow, the actor clicks on the "Chi tiết" (View Details) eye icon at the end of an order row. The system navigates the user to the detailed view of that specific order. |  |  |

#### **2.3.4 UC-B2C04 | Process Shipping Waybills**

| Primary Actors | Warehouse Staff | Secondary Actors | External E-commerce API |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a sales staff, I would like to request tracking numbers from the platforms so that packaged orders are ready for shipping. |  |  |
| **Preconditions** |   |  |  |
| **Postconditions** |   |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** |   |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | **Step 4a \- API Generation Error:** If the carrier API fails to generate a tracking number (e.g., due to an invalid customer address or missing phone number), the system displays an error message and flags the specific order as an "Exception" for manual handling. |  |  |

#### 

#### **2.3.5 UC-B2C05 | Manual Review & Select Warehouse**

| Primary Actors | Sales Staff | Secondary Actors | None |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a Sales Staff, I want to manually review risky or pending orders and assign them to a specific warehouse branch so that decrease risk for my shop |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | The Sales Staff is logged in with active permissions to process and modify order lifecycles. The local database contains existing order records waiting for approval (Chờ duyệt) Access the order detail screen Operational Warehouse Branches (e.g., Kho Hà Nội, Kho TP.HCM, Kho Đà Nẵng) are properly configured within the system settings  |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The order has been successfully processed in the database and updated via the exchange API.  It is now on the waiting list.  The warehouse team can go to the "Print Operation Code" tab to print labels and start picking/packing.  |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | The Sales Staff navigates to the "Xử lý đơn hàng" (Order Processing) menu item on the left sidebar. The system defaults the view to the "Đơn cần duyệt" tab, rendering a list of unapproved orders whose assigned warehouse is currently marked as Chưa chỉ định (Unassigned). The Sales Staff locates the target order and clicks the "Chi tiết" (View Details) eye icon at the end of the corresponding data row. The system displays the "CHI TIẾT ĐƠN HÀNG" (Order Detail) modal overlay. The Sales Staff reviews the order items and customer delivery information, then clicks the \*"Chọn Chi nhánh Kho xuất hàng " (Select Warehouse Branch) dropdown selector. The system displays the list of available active fulfillment warehouses. The Sales Staff selects the optimal warehouse branch (e.g., Kho Hà Nội). (Optional) The Sales Staff enters operational notes into the "Ghi chú phê duyệt / Từ chối lý do" text field. The Sales Staff clicks the "\[ DUYỆT ĐƠN & GIAO VIỆC KHO \]" action button. The system closes the modal layout, freezes modifications to the order locally, and automatically triggers an API call to the external E-commerce marketplace to request a Tracking ID. Upon successfully receiving the tracking data from the external API, the system automatically: Updates the Tracking ID on the order record (replacing the Chưa cấp tracking placeholder in the list view). Transitions the order status to "Awaiting Shipping Label Printing". Moves the order row from the "Đơn cần duyệt" tab into the "Chờ in mã vận đơn" tab. |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | **Alternative Flow A: Missing Warehouse Selection** At step 9 of the Basic Flow, the Sales Staff clicks "\[ DUYỆT ĐƠN & GIAO VIỆC KHO \]" while the warehouse dropdown remains unselected (-- Chọn kho xuất hàng \--). The system blocks the submission, highlights the \*"Chọn Chi nhánh Kho xuất hàng " field in red, and displays an error message: “Please select a warehouse branch before approving the order.” The flow returns to step 5 of the Basic Flow. **Alternative Flow B: Canceling Manual Review Operation** At any point prior to step 9 of the Basic Flow, the Sales Staff decides to cancel the manual review process. The Sales Staff clicks either the "Đóng" (Close) button at the bottom right or the close "X" icon in the top-right corner of the modal screen. The system closes the modal window, discarding any uncommitted dropdown choices, and returns to the pending order grid view.  |  |  |

#### 

#### **2.3.6 UC-B2C06 | Sync RTS API (Ready to Ship)**

| Primary Actors | Warehouse Staff | Secondary Actors | External E-commerce API |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a warehouse Staff, I want to confirm that orders are fully packed by the warehouse and trigger the "Ready to Ship" (RTS) API so that the E-commerce platform can dispatch the logistics carrier for pickup. |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | The Warehouse Staff has completely picked, packed, and updated the internal order status to "Packed" (UC-WH05). |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The order status on the E-commerce platform is updated to "Ready to Ship", and the logistics carrier is notified to pick up the parcels. |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | **Confirm Ready to Ship**  1\. Sales Staff accesses the Order List screen and filters for orders with the "Packed" status.  2\. Staff selects the packed orders and clicks the "Ready to Ship" (RTS) button.  3\. System calls the external E-commerce API to send the RTS confirmation payload.  4\. External API successfully updates the status and triggers the shipping carrier (3PL) to pick up the parcels.  5\. System updates the internal order status to "RTS / Waiting for Pickup".  6\. System displays a success toast message. |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | **Step 4a \- Synchronization Failure:** If the API connection times out or the platform rejects the RTS request (e.g., platform policy violation), the system prompts a warning banner and allows the Sales Staff to retry the synchronization later. |  |  |

## 

#### 2.3.7 UC-B2C07 | Update Order Delivery via Webhook

| Primary Actors | WMS System (Automated Background Process) | Secondary Actors | Shipping Carrier Webhook (ĐVVC) |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As an automated background process, the system continuously listens to shipping carrier webhooks to update order delivery journey statuses in real-time without requiring manual tracking by the Sales Staff. |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | The order has been picked up by the shipping carrier and possesses a valid tracking number. |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The order delivery status is continuously updated (e.g., Shipping, Delivered, Failed Delivery) in the local database. |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | **Auto-Update Delivery Status**  1\. The shipping carrier scans the package at a transit hub or delivery point.  2\. The carrier's system sends a webhook payload to the OMS.  3\. The WMS System intercepts the webhook and identifies the local Order ID via the tracking number.  4\. The WMS System updates the order status (e.g., to "Delivering" or "Delivered"). 5\. The system logs the status change event. |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | **Step 4a \- Auto-Complete Order:** If the status is updated to "Delivered", the system initiates a countdown. If no return/refund request is raised by the customer within a defined period (e.g., 3 days), the WMS System automatically updates the status to "Completed" to finalize revenue recognition.   **Step 4b \- Failed Delivery (Boom Hàng):** If the status is "Failed Delivery", the system updates the order and notifies the Warehouse team to prepare for receiving the physical RMA return package (UC-WH08). |  |  |

## 

#### 2.3.8 UC-B2C08 | Dispute Platform RMA

| Primary Actors | Sales Staff | Secondary Actors | External E-commerce API |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a Sales Staff, I want to review return requests and upload video evidence to the E-commerce platform to dispute unwarranted returns (e.g., damaged by carrier, fake returns) and claim compensation. |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | The physical return package has been received and isolated in the "Dispute Zone" by the Warehouse Staff (UC-WH08). |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The dispute claim is successfully submitted to the platform. Inventory is appropriately adjusted based on the platform's final resolution. |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | **Submit RMA Dispute**  1\. Sales Staff accesses the RMA Processing screen.  2\. System fetches and displays the pending return/refund requests initiated by customers on the E-commerce platforms.  3\. Staff reviews the request details and inspects the physical condition flagged by the Warehouse team.  4\. Staff uploads packing/unboxing video evidence or photos.  5\. Staff clicks "Submit Dispute".  6\. System calls the external E-commerce API to push the evidence payload to the platform.  7\. System updates the internal status to "Disputing" and waits for the platform's resolution. |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | **Step 7a \- Dispute Won (Compensated):** If the platform rules in favor of the seller, the status is updated to "Compensated". The damaged physical item remains in the Dispute Zone for scrap/disposal processing.  **Step 7b \- Accept Return (Seller's Fault):** If the return is valid, the staff clicks "Accept Return". The system updates the status to "Returned" and allows the Warehouse to restock the item if it is intact. |  |  |

#### 

#### 2.3.9 UC-B2C09 | Cancel B2C Order

| Primary Actors | Sales Staff | Secondary Actors | External E-commerce API |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a sales staff, I want to cancel a pending order because the warehouse has run out of stock. |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | The Sales Staff is logged in with active permissions to process and modify order lifecycles. The local database contains existing order records waiting for approval (Chờ duyệt) Access the order detail screen  |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The status of the target order is modified to a canceled/rejected state inside the enterprise resource planner database. The main order data grid on the parent page dynamically drops the affected item row from the pending queue. Update status order to e-commerce platform |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | The Sales Staff navigates to the "Xử lý đơn hàng" (Order Processing) menu sidebar item. The system defaults the view to the "Đơn cần duyệt" (Orders Awaiting Approval) tab, rendering a list of unapproved orders . The Sales Staff locates the target order and clicks the "Chi tiết" (View Details) eye icon at the end of the corresponding data grid row. The system overlays the "CHI TIẾT ĐƠN HÀNG" (Order Detail) modal window over the current screen. The Sales Staff reviews the order details and types a clear cancellation justification into the "Ghi chú phê duyệt / Từ chối lý do" text field . The Sales Staff clicks the "\[ TỪ CHỐI ĐƠN \]" action button. The system calls the respective E-commerce platform's API (Shopee, Lazada, TikTok) to request the order cancellation/rejection. The system receives a success response from the platform API, updates the local order status to "Canceled/Rejected", and closes the modal view. The system refreshes the background order list, decrementing the notification badge count  |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | **Alternative Flow A: Empty Cancellation Reason** At step 6 of the Basic Flow, the Sales Staff clicks "\[ TỪ CHỐI ĐƠN \]" without entering a reason in the mandatory text field. The system blocks the submission and displays an inline validation message requesting a justification comment. The flow returns to step 5 of the Basic Flow. **Alternative Flow B: Abandoning Cancellation Action** At any point prior to step 6 of the Basic Flow, the Sales Staff decides not to proceed with the cancellation. The Sales Staff clicks the **"Đóng"** (Close) button at the bottom right or the close **"X"** icon in the upper-right corner of the modal layout. The system discards all modifications, closes the modal window, and restores the main order list view without modifying data. |  |  |

## 

#### 2.3.10 UC-B2C10 | Sync Inventory to Channels API

| Primary Actors | WMS System (Cronjob) | Secondary Actors | External E-commerce API |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a background cronjob, the system periodically calculates and pushes real-time inventory updates to all connected sales channels to prevent overselling based on the defined safety buffer stock. |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | Master SKUs are mapped to E-commerce SKUs, and the API connections are active. |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The inventory levels on external platforms exactly reflect the local available inventory minus the predefined safety buffer. |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | **Sync Inventory to Platforms** 1\. The WMS System (Cronjob) triggers the synchronization process automatically at predefined intervals or upon any stock-change event (BR-01). 2\. System calculates the push quantity for each SKU: Push\_Qty \= qty\_available \- Buffer\_Stock. 3\. System formats the synchronization payload for the respective E-commerce platforms.  4\. System calls the external E-commerce APIs to update the stock quantities. 5\. External APIs return a "200 OK" success response. 6\. System logs the successful synchronization event in the background. |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | **Step 2a \- Zero Stock (Buffer Reached):** If the calculated Push\_Qty is less than or equal to 0 (because the actual stock has reached the safety buffer threshold), the system automatically pushes a quantity of "0" to the platforms to halt further sales and prevent overselling (BR-02).   **Step 4a \- API Rate Limit / Timeout:** If the external API is unresponsive or rate-limited, the system queues the failed payload and retries during the next cronjob cycle. |  |  |

## 

#### 2.3.11 UC-B2C11 | Manage Product Category Tree

| Primary Actors | Business Manager | Second Actors | None |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a business manager, I want to build, view, and maintain a hierarchical product category tree (up to 3 levels deep), so that products can be logically organized and categorized for multi-channel sales mapping and SKU generation. |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | The Business Manager must be logged into the internal system with an authorized `MANAGER` role and must have access to the Category Management page at `/business/categories`. |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The category tree is updated accordingly. Any newly created category is immediately visible to both Business Managers and Warehouse Staff. Deactivated categories remain in the database but are hidden from Warehouse Staff when creating new SKUs. |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | **6.1 — View Category Tree** Business Manager navigates to the Category Management page at `/business/categories`. System loads all categories (active and inactive) from the database and renders an interactive tree. Each tree node displays the category name, code badge, and action buttons. Business Manager can expand or collapse any node to show or hide its children. **6.2 — Create a New Category** Business Manager clicks the **"+"** button on an existing category node to add a child, or clicks **"Thêm danh mục"** to add a root-level category. System expands the parent node and displays an inline creation form with fields: `categoryCode` (3-4 uppercase alphanumeric characters, required), `categoryName` (required), and `description` (optional). Business Manager fills in the required fields and clicks **Save**. System validates the `categoryCode`: must be globally unique and within the 3-level tree depth limit. System saves the category as active (`active = true`) with `isImmutable = false`. The `levelDepth` is auto-computed from the parent. The new category appears immediately in the tree under its parent. **6.3 — Edit an Existing Category** Business Manager clicks the **Edit** (pencil) button on a category node. System displays an inline edit form pre-filled with the current values. The `categoryCode` field is read-only if the code has already been locked (`isImmutable = true`). Business Manager modifies the name, description, and/or parent assignment, then clicks **Save**. System validates: the code must remain 3-4 uppercase alphanumeric characters and unique; the new parent must not exceed level 3; the category cannot be its own parent or have a descendant as parent. System saves the update. If `categoryCode` was changed, `isImmutable` is set to `true` (code becomes permanently locked). `levelDepth` is recomputed based on the new parent. The tree view updates to reflect the changes. **6.4 — Delete a Category** Business Manager clicks the **Delete** (trash) button on a category node. System displays an inline confirmation dialog: If the category has **no products**: a red "Xóa" button is shown, confirming a hard delete. If the category **has products**: an orange "Ngung hoat dong" button is shown, confirming a soft delete only. Business Manager confirms the action. **Hard delete path**: System permanently removes the row from the `categories` table. **Soft delete path**: System sets `active = 0`. The category remains in the database but is hidden from Warehouse Staff in SKU creation screens. The tree view updates accordingly. |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | `categoryCode` already exists → *"Ma dinh danh da ton tai."* `categoryCode` invalid format (not 3-4 uppercase alphanumeric) → *"Ma dinh danh phai la 3-4 ky tu, chi gom chu hoa va so (VD: EYE, SUN)."* Invalid parent (self, descendant, or exceeds level 3\) → *"Danh muc khong the la danh muc cha cua chinh no."* Attempt to change `categoryCode` when `isImmutable = true` → *"Ma dinh danh da bi khoa, khong the sua."* Delete category that has products → Auto soft delete, *"Danh muc da co san pham. Da ngung hoat dong."* |  |  |

### **2.4 Analytics**

#### **2.4.1 UC-RPT01 | View Unified Analytics Dashboards**

| Primary Actors | Business Manager | Secondary Actors | None |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | :---: |
| **Description** | As a Business Manager, I want to view visualized data, revenue pie charts, and top-selling product reports so that I can analyze omnichannel performance and make informed business decisions regarding restocking or marketing. |  |  |
| **Preconditions** | The actor must be successfully logged into the Business Manager Dashboard. |  |  |
| **Postconditions** | The manager receives actionable insights to support strategic business decisions. |  |  |
| **Normal Sequence/Flow** | **View and Filter Analytics** 1\. Business Manager accesses the Revenue Report (Analytics Dashboard) screen. 2\. System fetches real-time sales, inventory, and revenue data from the database. 3\. System visualizes the data, displaying revenue proportion by channel using pie charts and ranking top-selling products. 4\. Manager applies data filters based on specific sales channels (e.g., Shopee, Lazada) or date ranges (e.g., 7 days, 30 days). 5\. System recalculates and refreshes the dashboard components instantly based on the selected criteria. |  |  |
| **Alternative Sequences/Flows** | **Step 5a \- No Data Available:** If there are no sales transactions within the selected date range or filtered channel, the system displays an empty state illustration with a "No data available for this period" message to prevent chart rendering errors. |  |  |

## 

## **3\. Functional Requirements** {#3.-functional-requirements}

*\[Provide descriptions about the system’s functions/screens. The functions/screens are grouped by the system features, and even sub-features if needed. For the screens, you need to provide the screen layouts (mock-up screens) and relevant specifications if needed\]*

### **3.1 Warehouse Management** {#3.1-warehouse-management}

##### **3.1.1 Warehouse Receipt Management**

*![][image13]*

This screen allows the Warehouse Staff to:

* Monitor Inbound Dashboard: Oversee incoming supplier shipments, track real-time fulfillment progress, and audit cumulative weekly stock-in metrics.  
* Manage Goods Receipt Notes (GRN): Process structural inbound vouchers from initial creation, cargo arrival tracking, down to final physical ledger updates.  
* Execute Physical Stock-Ins: Record actual receiving quantities directly against supplier purchase orders, updating real-time inventory counts and generating ledger entries upon confirmation.  
* Track Supplier Performance: Monitor specific line item delivery statuses to evaluate fulfillment completeness (items successfully stocked-in vs. items ordered)

**Field Description**

| Field Name | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| GRN Monitoring Dashboard  | Navigation Tab. Switches the view to the primary Goods Receipt Note (GRN) monitoring dashboard grid.  |
| Supplier Cost & Purchasing Rate  | Navigation Tab. Switches the view to the system interface for managing supplier cost parameters and purchasing rate structures.  |
