| Pending Inbound Vouchers  | Dashboard Card. Displays the total number of active inbound vouchers where stock is currently in transit or awaiting arrival from suppliers.  |
| GRN Documents In Physical Counting  | Dashboard Card. Indicates the exact number of GRN documents currently undergoing physical counting and offloading on the warehouse floor.  |
| Completed Inbound Vouchers (This Week)  | Dashboard Card. Displays the cumulative count of successfully closed and ledger-posted inbound vouchers within the current calendar week.  |
| Unique SKUs Received (This Week)  | Dashboard Card. Highlights the absolute cumulative volume of unique product line items (SKUs) successfully recorded into warehouse inventory over the past week.  |
| GRN Code / Supplier Name Filter  | Search Bar. Allows users to input partial or complete alphanumeric strings to instantly filter the inbound list by specific GRN codes or Supplier names.  |
| Create New GRN  | Action Button. Opens a configuration window form to initialize a brand-new internal Goods Receipt Note tracking voucher.  |
| Launch Receiving Interface  | Contextual Action Button. Available for active shipments; directly launches the receiving interface grid to let staff input counted physical stocks and finalize ledger updates.  |

##### **3.1.2 Warehouse Release Form Management**

![][image14]

This screen allows the Warehouse Staff to:

* Monitor Outbound Dashboard: Oversee pending outbound orders received from Sales Staff, evaluate staging workloads, and track active picking, packing, and dispatch fulfillment cycles.  
* Execute Picking and Packing Workflows: Coordinate physical fulfillment sequences by assigning and executing discrete item picking and packing tasks directly on the warehouse floor.  
* Manage Disposal/Scrap Issues: Initialize specialized disposal or scrap issue notes to clear damaged, expired, or non-sellable stock from physical inventory locations.  
* Track Fulfillment Completions: Monitor real-time row-level status changes to verify matching item physical picking balances before completing final dispatch procedures.

**Field Description**

| Field Name | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| Pending Pick-Pack Count  | Dashboard Card. Displays the total number of outbound orders successfully authorized and queued, awaiting physical pick-pack assignments.  |
| In-Progress Pick-Pack Count  | Dashboard Card. Shows the number of outbound orders currently undergoing active item selection or packaging operations on the floor.  |
| Awaiting Carrier Pickup Count  | Dashboard Card. Indicates the exact count of fully packed consignments currently aggregated in the staging zone awaiting courier pickup.  |
| Dispatched Today Count  | Dashboard Card. Displays the total number of outbound delivery documents successfully dispatched and cleared from the warehouse within the current working day.  |
| Urgent Pending Orders Banner  | System Notification Banner. Highlights the presence of urgent pending outbound orders that require immediate staff assignment to prevent dispatch delays.  |
| DO / SO Code Search Bar  | Search Bar. Allows users to input alphanumeric strings to filter the outbound order list by a specific Delivery Order code (`DO code`) or Sales Order code (`SO code`).  |
| Create Delivery Order Button  | Action Button. Opens a configuration view form to manually initialize a standard outbound Delivery Order tracking voucher.  |
| Create Scrap / Disposal Button  | Action Button (Danger Mode). Opens a configuration form to register a scrap or stock disposal voucher to clear dead inventory from system registers.  |
| Barcode Scanner Button  | Action Button. Activates device-linked barcode scanning hardware to quickly locate, pick, or update specific outbound documents via serial tags.  |
| Pick Fulfillment Status Block  | Fulfillment Status Block. Tracks picking completeness by comparing currently picked quantities against target requirements within that voucher line (e.g., `0/3` or `1/1`).  |
| Contextual Workflow Action Button  | Contextual Action Button. Adapts contextually to the document's state to guide processing workflows:  Trình duyệt BM: Routes a draft order to the Business Manager for formal authorization.  Bắt đầu Pick: Initiates the product location routing plan for floor staff selection. Xác nhận đóng gói: Finalizes the packing step and prepares the line for carrier handoff. |

##### **3.1.3 Create and Review Inventory Forms**

![][image15]

This screen allows the Warehouse Staff to:

Monitor Stock Take Pipelines: Track active cycle counts, pending balance approvals, and completed stock audits to accurately reconcile discrepancies.

Audit Physical Stock Levels: Perform real-time stock alignment by comparing standard system inventory numbers directly against manual, on-the-floor physical counts.

Manage Discrepancies Dynamically: Inspect calculated unit variances (Delta) per product line item to identify missing or surplus assets instantly.

Execute Stock Adjustment Controls: Initiate new comprehensive warehouse counts, submit finalized audit rows for review, or apply direct balance adjustments based on user roles.

**Field Description**

| Field Name | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| Inventory Status Filter Tabs  | Filter Tabs. A quick-navigation segmentation bar that filters the master inventory rows by their processing stages: All ("Tất cả"), Counting ("Đang kiểm đếm"), Awaiting Approval ("Chờ phê duyệt"), and Approved & Balanced ("Đã duyệt & Cân bằng").  |
| Inventory Code / Audit Title Search Bar  | Search Bar. Allows users to input partial or complete alphanumeric strings to instantly search the tracking grid by Inventory Code or specific audit titles.  |
| Export Report Button  | Action Button. Triggers the system to extract and compile the inventory data sheet into a downloadable spreadsheet format (.csv/.xlsx) for administrative audits.  |
| Create Stock Audit Voucher Button  | Action Button. Opens a contextual creation form interface to register a brand-new internal physical stock audit voucher.  |
| Finalize & Submit for Approval Button  | Locks the active count data and routes the ticket to management for confirmation.  |
| Adjust & Post Balance Button  | Available for authorized roles to finalize and post physical ledger balance updates.  |

##### **3.1.4 RMA Returns Management (Warehouse)**

![][image16]

This screen allows the Warehouse Staff to:

* Monitor RMA Intake Dashboard: Oversee real-time return stages including pending quality checks, active grading queues, approved sellable stock-ins, and scrap isolation metrics.  
* Process Customer Returns & QC: Perform physical quality control (QC) inspections directly on the warehouse floor to classify returned items based on actual condition.  
* Route Inventory by Quality: Segment inbound returns by automatically routing functional items back to sellable storage locations or diverting defective units to scrap zones.  
* Track Return Rationale: Audit specific consumer-reported return reasons alongside professional QC inspector remarks to improve quality assurance.

**Field Description**

| Field Name | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| Awaiting QC Inspection Count  | **Dashboard Card.** Displays the total number of customer return shipments currently received at the facility but awaiting physical quality inspection.  |
| In-Progress QC / Pending Decision Count  | **Dashboard Card.** Shows the number of return requests currently undergoing active grading evaluation or pending an administrative decision path.  |
| Good — Restock Count  | **Dashboard Card.** Tracks the count of processed return vouchers where items successfully passed quality standards and are designated to restock standard inventory.  |
| Damaged — Scrap Count  | **Dashboard Card.** Highlights the count of processed return vouchers containing damaged units classified as waste and isolated for scrap disposal.  |
| Unprocessed Return Slips Warning Banner  | **System Notification Banner.** Provides a prominent contextual warning indicating the number of unprocessed return slips remaining, prompting staff to complete QC testing to maintain stock accuracy.  |
| RMA / SO Code / Customer Name Search Bar  | **Search Bar.** Allows users to input alphanumeric text strings to instantly filter return records by specific RMA codes, original Sales Order (SO code) numbers, or customer names.  |
| Advanced Filter Button  | **Action Button.** Opens an advanced filter criteria dropdown panel to sort records by date ranges, sales platforms, or physical warehouse zones.  |
| Original Sales Order Reference  | **Data Row Element.** Shows the corresponding reference number of the originating sale order linking the return back to its source transaction (e.g., `SO-2026-001390`).  |
| RMA Code  | **Data Row Element.** Displays the unique system-generated identity assigned to the Return Merchant Authorization request (e.g., `RMA-2026-0088`).  |
| Start QC Inspection Button  | **Contextual Action Button.** Launches the interactive grading entry screen to record individual product condition counts, pass/fail status, and final zone placements.  |

##### **3.1.5 Master SKU Catalog (Warehouse View)** 

![][image17]

This screen allows the Warehouse Staff to:

* **Monitor Inventory Dashboard:** view real-time key performance indicators (KPIs) of the warehouse, including total physical inventory, low stock alerts, and warehouse fill rate.  
* **View Master SKU List:** view the list of physical products managed in the warehouse, focusing on dimensions, weight, and current physical stock levels.  
* **Search and Filter Data:** look up specific items using the search bar, category dropdown, or advanced filters.  
* **Manage SKU Details:** add new SKUs (if permitted) or edit the physical attributes (dimensions, weight) of existing SKUs.  
* **Export Data:** export the current inventory list to a CSV file for physical stocktake or reporting.

**Field Description**

| Field Name | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| Total Master SKUs Card  | Dashboard Card.Displays the total number of unique Master SKUs currently managed in this warehouse facility. |
| Total Physical Inventory Card  | Dashboard Card.Displays the sum of all physical individual units across all SKUs currently stored in the warehouse. |
| Active SKUs Card  | Dashboard Card.Shows the count of SKUs that have the "Active" business status. |
| Low Stock Alerts Card  | Dashboard Card.Highlights the number of SKUs whose current physical inventory has fallen below their defined Minimum (Min) threshold. |
| Inactive SKUs Card  | Dashboard Card.Shows the count of SKUs that are temporarily suspended or marked as "Inactive". |
| Warehouse Fill Rate Card  | Dashboard Card.Displays the warehouse space utilization percentage. |
| Search Input Bar  | Data type: string.Allows the user to enter keyword(s) to search for products by "Mã SKU" (SKU Code) or "Tên sản phẩm" (Product Name). |
| Category Filter Dropdown  | Dropdown list.Initial value: "Tất cả" (All). Allows the user to quickly filter the data grid by product categories. |
| Advanced Filter Button  | Action button.Opens an advanced filter modal to query data by multiple combined criteria (e.g., status, stock range). |
| Export CSV Button  | Action button.Triggers the system to download the currently displayed grid data as a .csv file. |
| Add New SKU Button  | Action button.Redirects the user to the "Create Master SKU" form to register a new product into the system. |
| Edit SKU Details Icon  | Action icon/button.Opens the details screen of the selected SKU, allowing the warehouse staff to update physical attributes such as dimensions (L×W×H), weight, and Min/Max thresholds. |

##### **3.1.6 Stock Transfer Management** 

![][image18]

This screen allows the Warehouse Staff to:

Coordinate Internal Inventory Logistics: Coordinate and distribute internal physical stock between standard warehouse locations or isolate units into specialized defective, damaged, or disputed holding zones.

Monitor Operational Pipeline: Track real-time stock transfer execution workflows across multiple stages including drafts, pending authorizations, completed shipments, and cancellations.

Execute Immediate Transfers: Initialize new internal stock movement vouchers to rapidly re-allocate warehouse item counts based on quality or floor demand.

Audit Multi-Zone Dispatches: Review granular line details, identifying the exact origin source zone versus the targeted destination branch zone to prevent stock loss.

**Field Description**

| Field Name | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| Total Transfer Requests Card  | Dashboard Card. Displays the total aggregated number of stock transfer request vouchers registered within the current monitoring cycle.  |
| Pending Approval Transfers Card  | Dashboard Card. Shows the exact number of created transfer orders currently awaiting formal authorization.  |
| Completed Transfers Card  | Dashboard Card. Shows the number of successfully authorized and physically received transfer requests.  |
| Draft Transfers Card  | Dashboard Card. Displays the current count of work-in-progress stock transfer vouchers saved but not yet submitted for processing.  |
| Search Input Bar  | Search Bar. Allows users to input partial or complete alphanumeric strings to instantly filter the data grid by Transfer Code, SKU identity, Product Name, or specific Warehouse labels.  |
| Create Transfer Button  | Action Button. Opens a contextual input form interface to configure and initialize a new stock relocation request document.  |
| Quick Workflow Actions  | Direct quick-action shortcuts to advance the document state through authorization pipelines directly from the list.  |
| Delete Draft Icon  | Allows users to delete or discard a work-in-progress draft entry.  |
| View Transfer Logs Icon  | Launches an uneditable read-only view detailing the historic transfer logs.  |

##### **3.1.7 Import Price Configuration**  

![][image19]

This screen allows the Warehouse Staff to:

* **Monitor Inventory Valuation:** view dashboard metrics summarizing the active SKUs, the average import cost, and the total converted monetary value of the current physical inventory.  
* **Configure Unit Cost:** input and update the "Giá nhập" (Import Price / Unit Cost) for individual Master SKUs based on supplier invoices during the inbound process.  
* **Calculate COGS:** view the automatically calculated "COGS Tồn kho" (Cost of Goods Sold / Inventory Value) for each product line based on the current physical stock.  
* **Save Configurations:** save import price updates individually per row or perform a bulk save for all modifications on the screen.

**Field Description**

| Field Name | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| Active SKUs Count Card  | Dashboard Card.Shows the total number of product SKUs that are currently active and eligible for import price configuration. |
| Average Unit Cost Card  | Dashboard Card.Displays the calculated average unit cost across all active SKUs in the warehouse. |
| Total Physical Inventory Value Card  | Dashboard Card.Displays the total estimated monetary value of the current physical warehouse stock. |
| SKU Business Status Label  | Control Type: Label/Badge.Displays the current business status of the SKU (e.g., Active, Sắp hết, Inactive).Business Rule: If the status is "Inactive", the system automatically disables the "Giá nhập" input field and the "Lưu" button to prevent updates on deprecated items. |
| Import Price Input Field  | Control Type: Numeric Textbox.Mandatory: Yes.Data type: Decimal/Integer (Currency format).The unit cost of the product from the supplier. |
| Save Row Pricing Button  | Control Type: Action Button.Action: Saves the modified "Giá nhập" for that specific row.System Action: Upon saving, the system automatically recalculates and updates the "COGS Tồn kho" column using the formula: COGS=Import Price×Current Stock. |
| Bulk Save All Pricing Button  | Control Type: Primary Action Button.Action: Performs a bulk operation. The system scans the entire grid for modified rows, validates the inputted prices, and saves all changes to the database simultaneously. |

##### 

### **3.2 Sales Staff**

#### **3.2.1 Channel Product Management** 

![][image20]

This screen allows the Sales Staff to:

* **Monitor Channel Performance:** view quick summary metrics of product listings across all e-commerce platforms, including total active listings, out-of-stock items, and total inventory value.  
* **View Channel Products List:** view the detailed list of products currently mapped and sold on external channels (Shopee, TikTok, Lazada, Website), including specific Channel SKUs and platform prices.  
* **Filter and Search Listings:** easily navigate the product list by filtering down to a specific sales channel or searching by Master SKU, Channel SKU, or Product Name.  
* **Manage Listings:** add new products to specific channels or edit existing channel-specific configurations (such as platform-specific pricing or sync status).

**Field Description**

| Field | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| total product by channel card | Display the total number of products on all platforms. |
| currently for sale card | Display the total number of products currently being sold on the platforms. |
| Out of Stock Card  | Display the total number of products sold on the platform that are out of stock. |
| Allocated Inventory Value Card  | Display the total value of inventory products currently for sale across all platforms. |
| Platform Filter Dropdown  | Initial value: "Tất cả kênh" (All channels).Allows the Sales Staff to filter the data grid to show only product listings from a specific platform (e.g., Shopee only, TikTok only). |
| Search Input Bar  | Control Type: Textbox.Data type: String.Allows the user to input keyword(s) to search for specific listings by "Tên" (Product Name), "Mã SKU" (Master SKU)), or "SKU kênh" (Channel SKU). |
| Publish product Button  | Control Type: button.Action: Redirect users to pop-ups so they can upload an existing product to the platform for sale. |
| Edit product details Icon  | Control Type: button. Action: Open the details screen for the selected channel product, allowing the Sales Representative to update channel-specific attributes such as price, description, and images.. |

##### 

##### **3.2.1.1 New Channel Product** 

Step 1: Select Root SKU 

![][image21]

This step allows Sales Staff to:

* **Search for approved items:** Filter the internal inventory registry by entering keywords such as internal product titles or Master SKU alphanumeric codes.  
* **Select the target inventory asset:** Pick a unique, pre-approved system product via individual radio buttons to serve as the root item for the sync payload.  
* **Navigate the setup pipeline:** Advance to the next channel configuration stage once an item is locked, or cancel the publishing process to dismiss the overlay.

**Field Description**

| Field | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Search Root SKU Input  | Text Input Field. Filters the active list of system catalog items by matching keywords against product names or Master SKU values.  |
| Master SKU Selection Radio  | Interactive Radio Control. Select an approved SKU to proceed to the next step. |
| Continue Button  | Action Button. Advances the user to *Step 2: Chọn Sàn bán* once a valid root SKU selection has been registered.  |
| Cancel Wizard Button  | Action Button. If you cancel your registration, your information will not be saved and the registration window will close. |

Step 2: Select Destination Channel

![][image22]

This step allows Sales Staff to:

* **Verify the active root SKU:** Review the read-only summary card displaying the specific item name and SKU chosen during the initial step.  
* **Choose target marketplaces:** Select one or more target e-commerce platforms (Shopee, Lazada, TikTok Shop) using radio/checkbox selection interfaces to define the broadcast destinations.  
* **Navigate step milestones:** Proceed to the final step if at least one marketplace is selected, return to Step 1 to alter the root item, or close out the wizard entirely.

**Field Description**

| Field | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Platform Selection Group  | The multi-platform selection tabs allow you to choose the platform you want to use (Shopee, Lazada, TikTok Shop). |
| Continue / Next Step Button  | Action Button. Conditional. Advances to *Step 3: “Cấu hình sàn”* once at least one destination platform has been selected.  |
| Back / Previous Step Button  | Action button. Return to the original Step 1: “Select SKU” screen while keeping the session window open..  |
| Cancel Wizard Button  | Action Button. If you cancel your registration, your information will not be saved and the registration window will close. |

Step 3: Channel Configuration 

![][image23]

This step allows Sales Staff to:

* **Assign categories:** Choose the platform-specific storefront category tree mapping from a dynamic dropdown menu to match marketplace requirements.  
* **Set retail values:** Enter specific prices for the selected channel interface.  
* **Manage product media assets:** Set product images: Upload and arrange the store's visual multimedia assets.  
* **Execute live sync publishing:** Run the final background API upload to broadcast the compiled payload to the channel network, navigate backward to modify store selections, or abort the process.

**Field Description**

| Field | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Platform Category Dropdown  | Dropdown Menu. Mandatory. Selects the target category route native to the external marketplace. |
| Retail Price Input  | Numeric Input Field. Mandatory. Accepts numeric entries to define the specific marketplace listing value for the item.  |
| Product Media Upload  | Media Upload Component. Accepts up to 5 square images (JPEG/PNG format, recommended 800x800px). The first image auto-allocates as the storefront listing cover thumbnail.  |
| Publish / Sync Button  | Action Button (Primary). Commits all wizard parameters, triggers the external marketplace API push queue, and closes the wizard window upon success.  |
| Back / Previous Step Button  | Action Button. Returns the workflow back to *Step 2: Chọn Sàn bán* to alter channel properties without wiping text input fields.  |
| Cancel Wizard Button  | Action Button. Closes the wizard dialog and dismisses all changes configured across all three step modules.  |

##### **3.2.1.2 Price Configuration**

![][image24]

This screen allows Sales Staff to:

* **Monitor price synchronization status metrics:** Check top-level numeric status cards indicating active channel connections, monitored items, and locked products.  
* **Isolate targeted workspace channels:** Click on explicit platform tags (Shopee, TikTok, Lazada, or Website) inside the selection channel bar to instantly swap and isolate the active product pricing dataset beneath. The current active editing channel is visually displayed in the indicator box.  
* **Configure baseline pricing figures:** Input or update the custom standard retail value into the **RETAIL PRICE** numeric input box assigned to a specific channel product row.  
* **Configure promotional campaigns:** Input a dedicated discount amount into the **PROMO PRICE** numeric input box to establish time-sensitive or channel-exclusive active marketing rates.  
* **Review real-time profit margins:** Instantly monitor calculated margin values under the column, which automatically balances against the product's base cost.  
* **Save configuration modifications:** Click the “**Lưu” (Save)** button located at the end of a product row to lock the updated retail/promo values and dispatch a sync price payload to the selected platform's API network.

**Field Description**

| Field | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Channel Quick Selection Tabs  | Channel Quick Selection Bar. Renders interactive status buttons representing integrated platforms (Shopee, TikTok, Lazada, Website). Clicking a button shifts the grid view context to that specific channel.  |
| Standard Retail Price Input  | Numeric Input Field. Allows staff to manually type or update the standard selling price for the chosen product on the active channel.  |
| Promotional Price Input  | Numeric Input Field. Allows staff to manually type or update the active promotional/discounted price for the product on the active channel.  |
| Save Row Price Button  | Action Button. Commits individual row price adjustments and transmits the newly configured pricing payload directly out to the channel's API node.  |

#### **3.2.2 Order List**

![][image25]

This screen allows Sale Staff to:

* **Filter orders by lifecycle status:** Quickly toggle between different tabs to view orders grouped by their current workflow stage, including *All Orders*, *Pending Confirmation*, *Awaiting Carrier Pickup*, *Shipping*, *Delivered*, *Completed*, and *Returned (RMA)*.  
* **Perform rapid search:** Look up specific orders instantly by typing keywords such as Order ID, Tracking Number (Waybill ID), Customer Name, or Phone Number into the search input field.  
* **Apply advanced attribute filters:** Narrow down the order dataset using multiple dropdown menus, allowing them to filter by Sales Channel, Internal Status, Product Lines, or Shipping Carrier.  
* **View comprehensive order overview metrics:** Monitor essential order details at a glance within the main data grid.  
* **Access granular order configurations:** Click the dedicated action icon (eye icon) to open the **3.2.2 Order Details (View Only)** screen for a deep-dive review of a single transaction.

**Field Description**

| Field | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Order Lifecycle Status Tabs  | Status Tabs \- Interaction Bar. Allows users to switch between order lifecycle states to filter the data grid below instantly.  |
| Global Search Input  | Search Bar \- Text Input Field. Accepts Alpha-numeric characters to query specific Order IDs, Waybills, or  Customer Credential. |
| Advanced Attribute Filters  | Dropdown Filter \- Selector Menus. Provides multi-conditional filtering options including Sales Channel, Detailed Status, Product SKU, and Shipping Carrier.  |
| View Order Details Icon  | Action Icon \- Interactive Button (Eye Graphic). Triggers a navigational routing command to open the View-Only detail page for the selected row item.  |

##### **3.2.2.1 Order Details (View Only)**

![][image26]

This screen allows Sales Staff to:

* **Review immutable transactional data:** View comprehensive system logs pulled directly from e-commerce integrations without risking accidental adjustments to native platform payloads.  
* **Inspect Client & Payment Coordinates:** Audit full customer delivery addresses, buyer remarks, and itemized financial breakdowns including platform vouchers, shop discounts, and net shipping costs.  
* **Monitor SKU Mappings:** Cross-reference the system's Master SKUs directly against the corresponding connected Channel SKUs to verify logistical consistency.  
* **Track Logistical Timelines:** Follow chronological delivery events updated live via automated webhook integration pipelines established with active shipping carriers.

#### **3.2.3 Order Processing**

![][image27]

This screen allows Sales Staff to:

* **Monitor specific processing queues:** Toggle between distinct operational workflow stages that require manual attention or status monitoring:  
  * **Đơn cần duyệt (Orders Awaiting Approval):** View orders currently blocked due to inventory discrepancies, price checks, or manual warehouse assignments.  
  * **Chờ in mã vận đơn (Awaiting Waybill Printing):** Manage orders that have successfully requested a tracking ID from the carrier API and are ready for shipping label printing.  
  * **Chờ bàn giao ĐVVC (Awaiting Carrier Handover):** Track orders packed and ready in the staging area for courier physical pickup.  
  * **Hàng hoàn & Khiếu nại (Returns & Disputes):** Monitor failed delivery packages or customer platform returns requiring QA validation.  
* **Filter and search real-time operational data:** Input keywords (Order ID, Waybill, Customer Phone) in the comprehensive search field (2) to isolate a single transaction.  
  * Apply multi-select dropdown attributes (3) to drill down by *Sales Channel (Kênh bán)*, *Product Series (Sản phẩm)*, or *Shipping Carrier (ĐVVC)*.  
* **Perform row-level fulfillment tracking:** Audit the status of individual line items on the main data grid before executing bulk shipping operations.  
* **Trigger detail verification actions:** Click the dedicated action icon on any targeted record row to evaluate deeper verification protocols before advancing the item to the warehouse picking queues.

**Field Description**

| Field | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Processing Queue Tabs  | Processing Queue Tabs \- Interaction Tabs. Toggles the dataset beneath to display filtered lists specialized by immediate logistical action needed (*Pending Approval*, *Awaiting Print*, *Awaiting Pickup*, *Returns*).  |
| Global Search Input  | Global Search Input **\-** Text Input Field. Queries the active database partition for matches on alphanumeric strings like Order ID, tracking numbers, or customer names.  |
| Multi-Select Filters  | Dropdown Filters \- Selector Menus. Narrows visible rows based on structural conditions including Sales Channel, Product, and Logistics Provider.  |
| Inspect Order Icon  | Action Icon \- Interactive Button. Launches the detail verification utility to inspect order details, SKU availability, and address formatting validations.  |

##### 

##### **3.2.3.1  Order Details & Verification**

![][image28]

This screen allows Sales Staff to:

* **Audit fundamental customer and carrier metadata:** Review core incoming marketplace payload configurations including Buyer Identity, Contact Number, Designated Logistic Channels (e.g., *TikTok Shop Express*), Waybill Tracking Codes, and Full Delivery Addresses.  
* **Execute real-time cross-branch stock availability validation:** Inspect granular inventory balances per ordered SKU across the enterprise node mesh:  
  * View total system inventory pool allocations.  
  * Verify active safety stock thresholds and cross-docking availability.  
  * Identify missing quantities or partial allocations across secondary operational warehouse hubs.  
* **Track chronological pipeline events:** Trace transaction milestones sequentially from the initial platform webhook ingestion hook up through internal ready-for-fulfillment staging phases.  
* **Perform manual warehouse routing overrides and administrative resolution:** Take corrective actions on paused or blocked orders via targeted UI entry fields:  
  * Select alternative physical warehouses to release physical goods.  
  * Input custom internal execution logs or operational memos.  
  * Trigger direct order cancellation/rejection paths or dispatch the validated transaction directly down to the designated WMS packaging queue.

**Field Description**

| Field | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Fulfillment Branch Selector  | Dropdown Selector. **Mandatory.** Allows the operator to manually select the specific fulfillment branch or warehouse node to pack and ship the order.  |
| Internal Operational Remarks  | Multi-line Text Area. **Optional.** Accepts alphanumeric characters for internal operational remarks, audit trails, or customer call-back logs.  |
| Reject / Approve Actions  | Action Button.  (1) Reject Orders \-  Available by default. Cancels internal allocations, halts the processing workflow, and sends a rejection status update to the channel API. (2) Approve & Assign. Conditional. Only becomes visible and active after a warehouse option has been selected in field (1). Commits the warehouse routing, locks inventory allocation, and dispatches the order to the WMS packaging queues.  |
| Close Details Overlay Button  | Action Button. Exits the detailed overlay interface and returns the sales staff to the main processing queue without saving any uncommitted changes.  |

#### **3.2.4 SKU Mapping**

![][image29]

![][image30]

This screen allows Sales Staff to:

* **Monitor overview synchronization summary metrics:** View real-time aggregate summary cards at the top of the workspace including *Total Internal Master SKUs (Tổng Master SKU nội bộ)*, *Unmapped Channel SKUs (Mã Sản chưa ánh xạ)*, and *Successful Connections / Join Rows (Liên kết ánh xạ)*.  
* **Toggle between mapping states:** Switch views instantly between the **SẢN PHẨM CHƯA ÁNH XẠ (UNMAPPED SKUs)** tab to handle isolated marketplace items and the **DANH SÁCH ĐÃ ÁNH XẠ (MAPPED SKUs)** tab to audit active sync relations.  
* **Pull listing updates directly from platforms:** Trigger an explicit sync call using the **Kéo sản phẩm từ Sàn** button to immediately fetch recent product listings and variations from connected store accounts.  
* **Filter and search localized datasets:** Cleanly isolate data rows by inputting targeted alpha-numeric keywords into contextual text boxes (e.g., searching by storefront code/title in the Unmapped view, or Master SKU variables in the Mapped view).  
* **Execute manual linking operations:** Open a dedicated modal configuration interface via the **Ánh Xạ** action button to bind an unmapped storefront variation row directly to its valid core system Master SKU parent.

**Field Description**

| Field | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Unmapped SKUs Tab  | Navigation Tab. Displays a data grid containing all product variations fetched from marketplace stores that do not currently have a mapped Master SKU in the system.  |
| Fetch Products Button  | Action Button. Sends an API query to integrated e-commerce channels to sync and update the list of current storefront products.  |
| Search Unmapped Grid Input  | Text Input Field. Filters the active Unmapped grid view by matching string data against the channel's SKU code or platform product title.  |
| Map / Link SKU Action Button  | Action Button. Launches a configuration sub-window enabling the operator to assign and link the chosen Channel SKU row to an internal Master SKU.  |
| Mapped SKUs Tab  | Navigation Tab. Switches the screen view to display a consolidated grid layout showing all successfully linked product structures and multi-channel mappings.  |

##### 

##### **3.2.4.1 Mapping Form**

![][image31]

This screen allows Sales Staff to:

* **Review originating channel item details (Section 1):** View immutable metadata pulled from the marketplace storefront payload to confirm the item targeted for linking, which includes:  
  * **Kênh bán (Sales Channel):** The source platform brand logo and platform identifier name (e.g., *Shopee*).  
  * **Mã SKU sàn (Channel SKU):** The raw variant SKU string registered on the storefront (e.g., SHP-DNT-CB2).  
  * **Tên sản phẩm trên sàn (Channel Product Title):** The public title displayed on the platform (e.g., Combo 2 Lược chải tóc gỡ rối pastel).  
* **Establish internal stock associations (Section 2):** Connect the channel item to a core system product and configure its packing multiplier rules:  
  * Select a corresponding Master SKU barcode number or system canonical name from a searchable list.  
  * Establish an implicit operational inventory deduction multiplier ratio per channel unit sale.  
* **Commit or discard changes:** Save the newly configured sync pathway using the primary execution button or immediately close out the sub-window to roll back uncommitted changes.

### **3.3 User Authentication** {#3.3-user-authentication}

#### **3.3.1 User Login**

![][image32]

This screen allows the System User (Admin, Manager, Sales Staff, Warehouse Staff) to:

* Log into the system: enter their registered Email / Phone number and Password to access the omnichannel sales management system.

On the screen, s/he can also:

* Toggle password visibility: click the eye icon inside the password text box to show or hide the entered password characters for verification.  
* Remember login session: check the "Ghi nhớ đăng nhập" (Remember me) box to save the login session for future access without re-entering credentials.  
* Recover password: choose to go to the Password Reset screen by clicking the "Quên mật khẩu?" (Forgot password) link if they forget their login credentials.

**Field Description**

| Field  | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| Email/Phone Input Field  | Data type: String (valid email address or phone number format), max length of 100 characters.Required field. |
| Password Input Field  | Data type: String (masked characters by default), max length of 50 characters.Includes an eye icon to toggle the visibility of the password text.Required field. |
| Remember Login Checkbox  | Data type: Boolean (Checkbox). Initial state: Unchecked (False). |
| Forgot Password Link  | Action link.Clicking this link redirects the user to the Password Reset / Recovery screen. |

##### **3.3.1.1 Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) \- Channel Selection**  

![][image33]

This screen is presented to users immediately after they successfully input their username and password. It allows the user to select their preferred communication channel (Email or SMS) to receive the One-Time Password (OTP) required for the second layer of authentication.

**Field Description**

| Field  | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| Email OTP Channel Radio  | Control Type: Radio Button / Selectable Card.Mandatory: Yes (One option must be selected).Displays the user's registered email address in a masked format (e.g., vu\*\*\*9@gmail.com).System Logic: Selected by default if it is the primary contact method configured by the Admin. |
| SMS OTP Channel Radio  | Control Type: Radio Button / Selectable Card.Mandatory: Yes (Alternative option).Displays the user's registered phone number in a masked format (e.g., 09\*\*\*\*\*\*21). |
| Send Verification Code Button  | Control Type: Primary Button.Action: Submits the selected channel to the system.System Action: The backend generates a temporary 6-digit OTP, dispatches it via the chosen gateway (Email SMTP or SMS Provider), and seamlessly redirects the user to the OTP verification screen. |
| Return to Login Link  | Control Type: Action Link.Action: Cancels the current authentication flow.System Action: Destroys the temporary session data and redirects the user back to the primary login screen. |

##### 

##### **3.3.1.2 Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Verification** 

#### **![][image34]**

This screen adds an essential secondary layer of security to the authentication process. After successfully validating their username and password, users are redirected to this screen to verify their identity using a One-Time Password (OTP) before gaining access to the OmniCore system.

**Field Description**

| Field  | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| Dispatched Channel Info  | Control Type: Text / Read-only.Displays the destination channel where the OTP was dispatched (e.g., Email or SMS).Security Rule: The system must mask the email address or phone number (e.g., vu\*\*\*9@gmail.com) to protect User Personally Identifiable Information (PII) from shoulder surfing. |
| OTP Success Alert Banner  | Control Type: Alert Banner.Provides immediate visual feedback confirming that the system has successfully generated and dispatched the OTP to the user's destination. |
| 6-Digit OTP Input Group  | Control Type: Numeric Textbox Group (6 slots).Mandatory: Yes.Data type: Integer (Exactly 6 digits).Allows the user to input the received OTP. For optimal UX, the cursor should automatically advance to the next slot as each digit is typed. |
| Verify Account Button  | Control Type: Primary Button.Action: Submits the entered OTP to the backend for validation.System Logic: If the OTP is correct and unexpired, the system grants an access token and redirects the user to the Dashboard. If incorrect, it displays an inline error message (e.g., "Mã OTP không chính xác"). |
| Resend Verification Code Link  | Control Type: Action Link.Action: Invalidates the previous OTP and triggers the system to send a new verification code.Business Rule: To prevent spam and API abuse, this action should implement a cooldown timer (e.g., disabled for 60 seconds after clicking). |
| Change Authentication Method Link  | Control Type: Action Link.Action: Redirects the user to a fallback screen to select an alternative 2FA method (if the user has configured multiple methods, such as SMS, Authenticator App, or an alternative email) in case they cannot access the primary channel. |

##### 

#### **3.3.2 User Profile**

![][image35]

This screen allows any authenticated User (e.g., Sales Staff, Warehouse Staff, Manager) to:

* **View Personal Profile:** view their current account information including their registered email and assigned system role.  
* **Update Contact Details:** edit their display name and phone number to keep their internal contact information up to date.  
* **Change Password:** securely update their login password by verifying their current password and entering a new one.

**Field Description**

| Field Name | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| Display Name Input  | Control Type: Textbox.Mandatory: Yes.Data type: String.Allows the user to update their display name in the system. |
| Registered Email Field  | Control Type: Textbox (Read-only).Data type: String.Displays the user's primary email address used for logging in.Business Rule: This field is disabled. Users cannot change their own email address to prevent login lockout; this requires System Admin intervention. |
| Contact Phone Number  | Control Type: Textbox.Mandatory: Yes.Data type: String (Phone format).Allows the user to update their contact phone number. |
| Assigned System Role Label  | Control Type: Textbox (Read-only).Data type: String.Displays the current permission level/role assigned to this user by the Admin. This field is disabled for editing. |
| Current Password Input  | Control Type: Password Textbox.Mandatory: Conditional (Only required if the user wants to change their password).Data type: String (Masked).Required to authenticate the user before permitting a password change. |
| New Desired Password Input  | Control Type: Password Textbox.Mandatory: Conditional.Data type: String (Masked).The new desired password.Business Rule: The system should enforce password complexity policies (e.g., minimum 8 characters, including numbers and special characters). |
| Confirm New Password Input  | Control Type: Password Textbox.Mandatory: Conditional.Data type: String (Masked).Must exactly match the value entered in the "Mật khẩu mới" field (6). |
| Save Changes Button  | Control Type: Primary Button.Mandatory: Yes.Action: Submits the form data.System Action: The system validates the inputs. If the user filled in the password fields, it verifies the "Current Password" against the database and checks if the new passwords match. Upon successful validation, it updates the user's record and displays a success toast message (e.g., "Cập nhật thông tin thành công"). |

##### 

### **3.4 System Administration** {#3.4-system-administration}

#### **3.4.1 User & Role Management (System Admin View)** 

![][image36]

This screen allows the System Admin to:

* **Monitor User Metrics:** view a quick summary of account statuses across the entire system via dashboard cards.  
* **View User List:** view the comprehensive list of all internal staff accounts, including their contact info, assigned roles, branch locations, and last login timestamps.  
* **Search and Filter Accounts:** quickly locate specific employees using the search bar or filter the data grid by specific roles.  
* **Manage User Accounts:** add new employees to the system, edit existing user profiles and role assignments, or instantly grant/revoke system access.

**Field Description**

| Field Name | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| Search Input Bar | Data type: string. Allows the admin to input keyword(s) to search for specific users by Name or Email. |
| Role Filter Dropdown | Dropdown list. Initial value: "Tất cả Vai trò" (All Roles). Allows the admin to filter the data grid to show only employees holding a specific role (e.g., ADMIN, MANAGER, SALES\_STAFF, WAREHOUSE\_STAFF). |
| Status Filter Dropdown | Dropdown list. Initial value: "Tất cả Trạng thái" (All Statuses). Allows the admin to filter users by their current account state (e.g., Hoạt động \- Active, Khóa \- Locked). |
| Add User Button | Action button. Redirects the admin to the user creation form to register a new user, set their credentials, and assign system roles. |
| Edit User Icon | Action icon/button. Opens the details screen of the selected user, allowing the admin to update profile information or modify role permissions. |
| Lock/Unlock Account Icon | Action icon/button. Triggers the process to instantly suspend or reactivate a user's system     access. |

##### 

##### **3.4.1.1 Add New Employee** 

![][image37]

This modal allows the System Admin to:

* **Input Employee Details:** enter personal and contact information (Name, Email, Phone) for the new internal staff member.  
* **Assign Branch & Role:** assign the employee to a specific physical branch and define their access permissions by assigning a system role.  
* **Trigger Account Creation:** create the account in the database and grant immediate access using a predefined default password.

**Field Description**

| Field Name | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| Back to List Link  | Navigation link. Located at the top with a left arrow. Instantly redirects the admin back to the main User & Role Management screen without saving. |
| Username Input  | Text field. Required (\*). Data type: string. Business Rule: This field displays the current username (e.g., "Trung") and is read-only / disabled during update to maintain data integrity. |
| Full Name Input  | Text field. Required (\*). Data type: string. Pre-filled with the user's current full name (e.g., "Đức Trung"), allowing modification. |
| Email Address Input  | Text field. Required (\*). Data type: string (email format). Pre-filled with the current work email (e.g., "ductrung3625@gmail.com"), allowing modification.  |
| Phone Number Input  | Text field. Optional. Data type: string (numeric format). Pre-filled if previously updated, otherwise shows placeholder "VD: 0912345678".  |
| Role Access Dropdown  | Dropdown list. Required (\*). Allows changing the user's authority level (e.g., currently selected: "WAREHOUSE\_STAFF — Nhân viên kho").  |
| Warehouse Assignment Dropdown  | Dropdown list. Conditional field. Appears or activates when specific roles like WAREHOUSE\_STAFF are selected, letting the admin assign a workspace (e.g., "Kho Hà Nội").  |
| Status Radio Buttons  | Radio buttons. Displays the current status (e.g., selected: "Đang hoạt động (Active)"). Allows toggling between Đang hoạt động (Active) and Đang khóa (Locked/Inactive).  |
| Cancel Button  | Action button. Discards any modifications made on this form and redirects back to the user list.  |
| Save Information Button  | Action button. Validates updated entries, applies changes to the user's profile database, and shows a success notification.  |

##### 3.4.1.1 Update New Employee 

![][image38]

This screen allows the System Admin to:

* **Modify Personal Profile:** Review and update the existing personal identification and contact details of the user.  
* **Adjust Roles, Assign Locations & Status:** Reconfigure the user's system role, assign a specific management location (e.g., warehouse), and modify their account operation status.  
* **Save or Discard Changes:** Commit the updated details to the database or cancel the action to retain original settings.

| Field Name | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| Back to List Link  | Navigation link. Located at the top with a left arrow. Instantly redirects the admin back to the main User & Role Management screen without saving. |
| Username Input  | Text field. Required (\*). Data type: string. Business Rule: This field displays the current username (e.g., "Trung") and is read-only / disabled during update to maintain data integrity. |
| Full Name Input  | Text field. Required (\*). Data type: string. Pre-filled with the user's current full name (e.g., "Đức Trung"), allowing modification. |
| Email Address Input  | Text field. Required (\*). Data type: string (email format). Pre-filled with the current work email (e.g., "ductrung3625@gmail.com"), allowing modification.  |
| Phone Number Input  | Text field. Optional. Data type: string (numeric format). Pre-filled if previously updated, otherwise shows placeholder "VD: 0912345678".  |
| Role Access Dropdown  | Dropdown list. Required (\*). Allows changing the user's authority level (e.g., currently selected: "WAREHOUSE\_STAFF — Nhân viên kho").  |
| Warehouse Assignment Dropdown  | Dropdown list. Conditional field. Appears or activates when specific roles like WAREHOUSE\_STAFF are selected, letting the admin assign a workspace (e.g., "Kho Hà Nội").  |
| Status Radio Buttons  | Radio buttons. Displays the current status (e.g., selected: "Đang hoạt động (Active)"). Allows toggling between Đang hoạt động (Active) and Đang khóa (Locked/Inactive).  |
| Cancel Button  | Action button. Discards any modifications made on this form and redirects back to the user list.  |
| Save Information Button  | Action button. Validates updated entries, applies changes to the user's profile database, and shows a success notification.  |

#### 3.4.2 API & Webhook Integration 

![][image39]

This screen allows the System Admin to:

* **Monitor Integration Health:** view real-time metrics of system connections, including the number of successful/failed channels, daily event volume, and average API latency.  
* **Manage Platform Connections:** configure credentials (App ID, API Secret) to authorize data exchange between the OMS and external e-commerce platforms (Shopee, TikTok Shop, Lazada, custom websites).  
* **Configure Webhooks:** set up endpoints to receive real-time event triggers (e.g., order created, stock updated) from external platforms.  
* **Test and Troubleshoot:** run connection tests to verify API keys and view real-time webhook logs to debug data synchronization errors.

**Field Description**

| Field Name | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| Connected Platforms Ratio Card  | Dashboard Card.Displays the ratio of successfully connected external platforms out of the total configured platforms. |
| Failed Connections Card  | Dashboard Card.Highlights the number of channels currently experiencing connection drops, authentication failures, or endpoint errors. |
| Daily Webhook Volume Card  | Dashboard Card.Shows the total volume of webhook payloads (events) successfully received and processed by the system within the current day. |
| Average API Latency Card  | Dashboard Card.Displays the average response time (in milliseconds) for API requests and webhook acknowledgments. |
| Error Log Redirection Link  | Action link.Located inside the system alert banner. Clicking this link redirects the Admin to the detailed error log specifically filtered for the disconnected channel (e.g., Lazada) to investigate the root cause. |
| Add API Connection Button  | Primary Action button.Opens a dialog/form to select a new e-commerce platform provider and input API credentials to establish a new integration. |
| Test API Connection Button  | Action button.Triggers a sample API request (ping) to the provider's endpoint using the configured credentials to verify if the connection is valid and active. |
| Save API Credentials Button  | Action button.Saves any modifications made to the App ID, API Secret, or Webhook Endpoint fields into the database. |
| Refresh Webhook Logs Button  | Action button.Reloads the "Nhật ký Webhook gần đây" (Recent Webhook Logs) data grid to fetch the latest incoming events and HTTP status codes (e.g., HTTP 200, HTTP 401\) without refreshing the entire page. |
| Unmask API Secret Toggle  | Toggle icon button.Data type: String (masked by default).Allows the Admin to temporarily unmask the "APP SECRET / API KEY" field to view the raw character string for copying or verification. |

##### **3.4.2.1 Add New API Connection** 

![][image40]

This screen allows the System Admin to:

* **Select Target Platform:** choose the specific e-commerce platform or custom website to integrate with the OMS.  
* **Input API Credentials:** enter the authentication keys (App ID, API Secret) provided by the platform's developer console.  
* **Define API Endpoint:** specify the base URL for the integration.  
* **Save and Verify:** save the configuration and immediately trigger a test request to ensure the credentials and endpoint are valid.

**Field Description**

| Field Name | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| Target Platform Dropdown  | Control Type: Dropdown list.Mandatory: Yes.Allows the admin to select the target e-commerce platform (e.g., Shopee, Lazada, TikTok Shop, Custom Web) from predefined system options. |
| App ID / Partner ID Input  | Control Type: Textbox.Mandatory: Yes.Data type: String.The public identifier or partner ID registered on the external platform's developer console. |
| App Secret / API Key Input  | Control Type: Textbox (usually masked).Mandatory: Yes.Data type: String.The private authentication key. Business Rule: The system must encrypt this value before saving it to the database to ensure security. |
| API Base Endpoint URL Input  | Control Type: Textbox.Mandatory: Yes.Data type: String (URL format).The base URL provided by the platform for API requests. The system may auto-fill a default value based on the selected Platform (1). |
| Cancel Modal Button  | Control Type: Button.Mandatory: No.Action: Discards all entered data and closes the modal without making any changes to the system. |
| Save & Verify Connection Button  | Control Type: Primary Button.Mandatory: Yes.Action: Submits the form data. The system temporarily saves the configuration and sends an immediate test request to the specified endpoint. If successful, the connection is permanently saved and activated. If failed, the system displays an error message (e.g., Invalid API Key) and keeps the modal open for correction. |

### **3.5 Business Manager**

#### **3.5.1 Master SKU Catalog** 

![][image41]

![][image42]

This screen allows the Business Manager to:

* **Create Master SKU List:** view the comprehensive list of product root data (Master SKUs) in the system, including inventory levels and thresholds.  
* **Filter Master SKU List:** filter data quickly by approval statuses using the top tabs or by specific categories using the dropdown filter.  
* **Search Master SKUs:** enter keyword(s) to search for specific products by their SKU code or product name.  
* **Review and Approve SKUs:** perform approval workflows (Approve/Reject) for newly created or updated SKUs.  
* **Export Data:** export the current list of SKUs to a CSV file for reporting purposes.

Field Description

| Field Name | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| All SKUs Tab  | "Tất cả" (All) Tab: Displays all SKUs in the system regardless of their approval status. Shows the total count of items. |
| Create Master SKU | Create master sku modal for the system. |
| Search SKU Input Bar  | Search Box: Data type: string.Allows the user to input text to search by "Mã SKU" (SKU Code) or "Tên sản phẩm" (Product Name). |
| Category / Status Filter  | Filter Dropdown: Allows the user to filter the data grid by specific criteria (e.g., Category or Business Status). The default value is "Tất cả" (All). |
| Export Data to CSV Button  | Export CSV Button: Action button.Triggers the system to download the currently displayed data grid as a .csv file. |

#### **3.5.2 Report** 

![][image43]

![][image44]

![][image45]

![][image46]

This screen allows the Business Manager to:

Monitor Multi-Channel Sales Revenue: Track real-time sales revenue, cumulative transaction volumes, and performance changes over custom historical time windows.

Analyze Sales Channel Composition: Evaluate revenue distribution percentages and growth rates across different connected marketplaces and web channels.

Track Fulfillment Pipelines: Oversee the proportional distribution of transaction lifecycles, measuring total volumes from processing to shipping stages.

Audit Top Performing Catalog Items: Identify high-grossing stock items (SKUs) based on gross multi-channel revenue generation and growth trends.

**Field Description**

| Field Name | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| Time Window Selection Tabs  | Time Window Selection Tab. Filters all dashboard KPIs, charts, and grids by specific operational windows: 7 Days ("7 ngày"), 30 Days ("30 ngày"), 3 Months ("3 tháng"), 6 Months ("6 tháng"), or 1 Year ("1 năm"). *(Default active: 30 ngày)*.  |
| Refresh Data Metrics Button  | Action Button. Triggers an immediate system data re-fetch to update all charts and metrics with the latest transaction records.  |
| Export Offline Report Button  | Action Button. Extracts the summarized multichannel revenue statistics and exports them into an offline tabular report file (.csv/.xlsx).  |
| Revenue Trend Line Chart  | Multi-Line Trend Chart. Visualizes daily revenue fluctuations over the active time filter, color-coded by originating marketplace channels (*Shopee, TikTok, Lazada, Website*) across linear tracking baselines.  |
| Revenue Distribution Pie Chart  | Pie Chart Distribution. Displays a proportional breakdown of total gross sales generated per channel, detailing matching percentage weights and exact numeric values (e.g., *Shopee 44% \- 14.7M, TikTok 30% \- 10.1M, Lazada 17% \- 5.5M, Website 9% \- 2.9M*).  |
| Order Status Pipeline Donut Chart  | Donut Chart & Horizontal Bars. Breaks down the active order fulfillment pipeline by operational milestone states, tracking precise counts and ratios: Đã giao: Shipped and completed orders (e.g., 68% \- 10.445 đơn). Đang giao: Shipments in-transit via third-party logistics (e.g., 17% \- 2.611 đơn). Chờ xử lý: New pending orders in the queue (e.g., 9% \- 1.382 đơn).  Đã huỷ: Orders cancelled by customer or system (e.g., 4% \- 614 đơn).  Hoàn hàng: Dispatched shipments rejected or returned (e.g., 2% \- 307 đơn). |
| Top Performing SKUs Link  | Navigation Link. Redirects the manager to the full-screen comprehensive product analytics ledger for in-depth SKU audits.  |

#### **3.5.3 Product Category Management** 

![][image47]

This screen allows the Business Manager to:

* **View Category Hierarchy:** visualize the entire product catalog structure through an interactive, multi-level tree view (Tree-view).  
* **Create Categories:** add new root categories (Level 1\) or nest new sub-categories under existing parent nodes.  
* **Edit Categories:** modify the names of existing categories or reassign their parent nodes to restructure the hierarchy.  
* **Delete Categories:** remove obsolete categories from the system.

**Field Description**

| Field Name | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| Create Root Category Button  | Control Type: Button.Action: Prepares the right-side form to create a new root category by clearing all inputs and setting the Parent Category (7) to "Không có" (None). |
| Toggle Child Categories Icon  | Control Type: Action Icon (Chevron/Folder).Action: Toggles the visibility of nested child categories under a specific parent node. |
| Add Nested Category Icon  | Control Type: Action Icon.Action: Selects the current node as the parent and opens the right-side form to create a new child category directly under it. |
| Edit Category Details Icon  | Control Type: Action Icon.Action: Loads the selected category's data (Name, Parent ID) into the right-side form for modification. |
| Delete Category Icon  | Control Type: Action Icon.Action: Triggers the deletion process.Business Rule: The system must prevent deletion if the category currently contains active Master SKUs or active sub-categories. A confirmation modal must be displayed before actual deletion. |
| Category Display Name Input  | Control Type: Textbox.Mandatory: Yes (indicated by \*).Data type: String.The public-facing display name of the product category. |
| Parent Category Dropdown  | Control Type: Dropdown list.Mandatory: No.Allows the Manager to map this category to a higher-level parent. Selecting "Không có (Cấp cao nhất)" makes it a Level 1 root category. |
| Calculated Category Level Badge  | Control Type: Label / Badge (Read-only).Data type: Integer.An auto-calculated indicator showing the depth of the category (e.g., Level 1, Level 2\) based dynamically on the selected Parent Category in field (7). |
| Cancel Form Button  | Control Type: Button.Mandatory: No.Action: Discards inputted data, clears the form, and cancels the current add/edit operation without saving to the database. |
| Save Category Form Button  | Control Type: Primary Button.Mandatory: Yes.Action: Submits the form. The system validates the inputs (e.g., checking for duplicate category names at the same level). Upon success, it saves the data, displays a success toast message, and dynamically refreshes the Tree-view (left panel) to reflect the changes. |

#### **3.5.4 Personnel Management**  

![][image48]

This screen allows the Business Manager to:

* **Monitor Subordinate Staff:** view dashboard metrics summarizing the total number of personnel under their supervision, including active and deactivated accounts.  
* **View Personnel Roster:** view a comprehensive list of operational staff (e.g., Sales Staff, Warehouse Staff), including their usernames, contact details, assigned roles, and branch locations.  
* **Search and Filter Data:** efficiently locate specific employees using the search bar (by Username, Name, Email, or Phone) or filter the grid by specific roles and statuses.  
* **Modify Staff Assignments:** edit existing staff profiles to update branch assignments or operational details, noting that account creation is strictly restricted.

**Field Description**

| Field Name | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| Total Supervised Staff Card  | Dashboard Card.Displays the total number of staff accounts currently managed or monitored by this Business Manager. |
| Active Supervised Staff Card  | Dashboard Card.Shows the count of supervised staff whose accounts are currently active. |
| Inactive Supervised Staff Card  | Dashboard Card.Highlights the number of supervised staff whose accounts are currently locked or disabled. |
| Search Staff Input Bar  | Control Type: Textbox.Data type: String.Allows the Manager to search the grid by entering a Username, Full Name, Email, or Phone Number. |
| Staff Role Filter Dropdown  | Control Type: Dropdown list.Initial value: "Tất cả vai trò" (All roles).Filters the data grid to display only staff members holding a specific role (e.g., Sales Staff). |
| Staff Status Filter Dropdown  | Control Type: Dropdown list.Initial value: "Tất cả trạng thái" (All statuses).Filters the data grid to display staff based on their current system status (Active or Inactive). |
| Creation Restriction Banner  | Control Type: Information Banner / Read-only Label.Displays a system rule informing the Manager that the permission to create new personnel accounts is strictly reserved for the System Admin (Maker). |
| Edit Operational Details Button  | Control Type: Action Button.Action: Opens a modal or detail screen allowing the Business Manager to update the staff member's operational details (such as reassigning them to a different branch).Business Rule: The Manager cannot edit the core authentication details (Username/Password) or change the Role to Admin. |
| Account Status Badge  | Control Type: Status Badge (Read-only).Displays whether the account is "Đang hoạt động" (Active) or "Vô hiệu hóa" (Inactive).Business Rule: Unlike the System Admin view, the Manager does not have a toggle switch here and cannot directly activate/deactivate accounts. |
| System Role Badge  | Control Type: Label / Badge (Read-only).Displays the specific system role assigned to the staff member (e.g., Warehouse Staff, Sales Staff). |

#### 

#### **3.5.4 Warehouse List Management** 

![][image49]

This screen allows the Business Manager to:

* **Monitor Warehouse Network:** view dashboard metrics summarizing the total number of physical warehouse branches and their current operational statuses (active vs. temporarily closed).  
* **View Warehouse Details:** view a comprehensive list of all warehouse locations, including their unique codes, names, full addresses, internal storage zones (e.g., Khu Hàng Thường, Khu Hàng Hỏng), and contact numbers.  
* **Search Facilities:** efficiently search for specific warehouses by code, name, or address using the search bar.  
* **Manage Warehouse Operations:** register new warehouse branches into the system, edit existing facility details, and toggle the operational status of any branch (Close/Reopen).

**Field Description**

| Field Name | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| Total Registered Warehouses Card  | Dashboard Card.Displays the total number of physical warehouse branches registered in the system. |
| Active Warehouses Card  | Dashboard Card.Shows the count of warehouses that are currently open and operational. |
| Closed Warehouses Card  | Dashboard Card.Highlights the number of warehouses that are temporarily suspended from operations. |
| Register New Warehouse Button  | Control Type: Primary Button.Action: Redirects the user to a form or modal to register a new physical warehouse location, including defining its address and internal storage zones. |
| Warehouse Status Badge  | Control Type: Label / Badge (Read-only).Displays the current operational status of the warehouse branch ("Đang hoạt động" or "Tạm đóng cửa"). |
| Edit Warehouse Details Icon  | Control Type: Action Icon.Action: Opens the detail screen, allowing the Manager to update the warehouse's basic information (name, address, phone) or configure its internal storage zones. |
| Toggle Open/Close Status Button  | Control Type: Action Button.Action: Instantly toggles the operational status of the selected warehouse.Business Rule: If the Manager attempts to click "Đóng cửa" (Close) on a warehouse that currently holds active inventory, the system should ideally prompt a warning modal advising them to transfer the goods (Điều chuyển kho) before closing the facility. |
| Search Warehouse Input Bar  | Control Type: Textbox.Data type: String.Allows the Manager to search the data grid by entering a Warehouse Code (Mã kho), Warehouse Name (Tên kho), or Address (Địa chỉ). |

#### **3.5.4 Multi-Warehouse Inventory** 

![][image50]

This screen allows the Business Manager to:

* **Monitor Inventory Health:** view high-level dashboard alerts identifying items that are critically low or approaching out-of-stock status across the entire warehouse network.  
* **View Multi-Location Stock:** view the detailed breakdown of physical inventory (Tồn vật lý) for each product Master SKU mapped to specific warehouse locations.  
* **Search, Filter, and Sort:** locate stock records using the search bar, filter by specific warehouses or stock statuses, and sort the data grid by physical stock quantities.  
* **Trigger Restock Alerts:** actively send notifications or restock requests to specific warehouse branches for items that have problematic stock levels.

**Field Description**

| Field Name | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| Critical Stock Alerts Card  | Dashboard Card.Shows the number of inventory lines where the physical stock has dropped to zero or a critically low level, requiring immediate business attention to prevent out-of-stock on sales channels. |
| Low Stock Threshold Alerts Card  | Dashboard Card.Highlights the count of inventory lines where the physical stock is approaching or has fallen below the predefined minimum threshold (Min Stock). |
| Total Inventory Lines Card  | Dashboard Card.Displays the total number of product-location combinations currently tracked in the system (e.g., 1 Master SKU stored in 3 different warehouses equals 3 inventory lines). |
| Search Inventory Input Bar  | Control Type: Textbox.Data type: String.Allows the Manager to quickly search the grid by entering a Master SKU code or Product Name. |
| Warehouse Branch Filter  | Control Type: Dropdown list.Initial value: "Tất cả kho" (All warehouses).Filters the data grid to display inventory data strictly for a selected warehouse branch. |
| Stock Health Status Filter  | Control Type: Dropdown list.Initial value: "Tất cả trạng thái" (All statuses).Filters the data grid based on stock health categories (Nghiêm trọng, Sắp hết, An toàn). |
| Physical Stock Sort Header  | Control Type: Button / Column Header Sort.Action: Toggles the sorting order of the data grid (Ascending/Descending) based on the numerical value in the "Tồn vật lý" (Physical Stock) column. |
| Send Restock Alert Button  | Control Type: Action Button.Action: Triggers an internal system notification or automated task sent directly to the Warehouse Staff of that specific facility, prompting them to verify physical stock, initiate an internal transfer, or prepare for inbound replenishment.Business Rule: This button is only visible/enabled for rows with "Nghiêm trọng" or "Sắp hết" statuses. |
| Health Status Badge  | Control Type: Label / Badge (Read-only).A color-coded visual indicator reflecting the stock health.System Logic: Evaluated dynamically by comparing the current "Tồn vật lý" against the Min stock threshold configured for that specific SKU. |

#### **3.5.4 Inventory Ledger & Approvals** 

![][image51]

This screen allows the Business Manager to:

* **Monitor Inventory Transactions:** view a comprehensive, centralized log of all warehouse documents across the system, including Inbound (Nhập kho), Outbound (Xuất kho), Stocktake (Kiểm kê), Internal Transfers (Chuyển kho), and Customer Returns (Hoàn hàng).  
* **Execute Maker-Checker Workflow:** act as the "Checker" to review and perform approval workflows (Approve or Reject) on pending inventory slips created by the Warehouse Staff ("Maker") to ensure data accuracy and prevent operational fraud.  
* **Search and Filter Documents:** quickly locate specific transaction records using categorized tabs or the search bar.  
* **Audit Document Details:** view the full details of any historical or pending transaction to verify item quantities, targeted storage zones, and creator information.

**Field Description**

| Field Name | Description |
| ----- | :---- |
| Document Category Filter Tabs  | Control Type: Tab Group.Action: Filters the data grid to show only specific types of inventory documents.Business Rule: The system displays red notification badges (e.g., 1, 2\) on these tabs to visually alert the Manager about the exact number of documents currently awaiting their approval in that specific category. |
| Search Document Input Bar  | Control Type: Textbox.Data type: String.Allows the Manager to search the ledger by entering a Document Code (Mã phiếu), Document Type (Loại chứng từ), or the Creator's Name (Người tạo). |
| View Full Document Details Icon  | Control Type: Action Icon.Action: Opens the detailed view of the selected document. The Manager can review the exact list of SKUs, quantities, and operational notes before making an approval decision. |
| Approve Document Action Icon  | Control Type: Action Icon.Action: Approves the pending document.System Logic: Clicking this changes the document status from "Chờ duyệt" (Pending) to "Đã duyệt" (Approved) or "Hoàn thành" (Completed). This action triggers the actual addition or deduction of physical stock in the system's database. This button is only visible for rows with a "Chờ duyệt" status. |
| Reject Document Action Icon  | Control Type: Action Icon.Action: Rejects the pending document.System Logic: Changes the status to "Từ chối" (Rejected) or "Đã hủy" (Cancelled). The system should typically prompt the Manager to input a rejection reason. The physical stock remains unchanged. This button is only visible for rows with a "Chờ duyệt" status. |

#### 

## **4\. Non-Functional Requirements** {#4.-non-functional-requirements}

### **4.1 External Interfaces** {#4.1-external-interfaces}

**User Interfaces:**

**Description:** The system provides a web-based user interface accessible via modern web browsers (e.g., Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, Safari,..). 

**Requirements:** The interface must follow Responsive Web Design (RWD) standards to display properly on various screen sizes, including desktops, laptops, and tablets.

**Hardware Interfaces:**

**Description:** The system interacts with physical hardware components located at the physical warehouse to speed up operations. 

**Requirements:**

- Barcode Scanner: The system must interface with standard USB or Bluetooth handheld barcode scanners. When a barcode is scanned in the "Inbound/Outbound" screen, the system must automatically capture the text input to identify the product SKU.  
- Receipt/Label Printer: The system must support sending print jobs to standard thermal label printers (via Windows/Mac print spoolers) to print shipping labels and product barcodes. 

**Software Interfaces :**

**E-commerce Platform APIs:** 

- The system integrates with external Open APIs of platforms like *Shopee Open Platform, Lazada Open Platform, and TikTok Shop Academy*.   
- Data exchanged: Product listings, inventory levels (stock update), and order details (customer info, shipping status). 

**Shipping Carrier APIs:**  The system connects to third-party logistics (3PL) providers (e.g., Giao Hàng Nhanh \- GHN, Giao Hàng Tiết Kiệm \- GHTK, Viettel Post) to push order data, calculate shipping fees, and retrieve tracking numbers (Waybill numbers). 

**Database Management System:** The system interfaces with a relational database (e.g., MySQL, PostgreSQL, or SQL Server) using standard database drivers (like JDBC) to store and retrieve master data. 

**Communications Interfaces:** 

**Network Protocols:** The system uses **HTTPS** (Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure) for all communications between the client (web browser) and the server, ensuring data encryption via SSL/TLS. 

**API Architecture & Data Format:** 

- Communication with E-commerce platforms and shipping APIs will be handled via **RESTful APIs**.   
- The standard data exchange format for all API payloads (requests and responses) must be **JSON** (JavaScript Object Notation). 

**Real-time Notifications:** The system utilizes **WebSockets** or **Webhooks** provided by E-commerce platforms to receive instant real-time updates when a new order is placed or cancelled on the store.

### **4.2 Quality Attributes** {#4.2-quality-attributes}

#### **4.2.1 Usability** 

**Description: T**he system must be intuitive, easy to navigate, and minimize human errors during intense warehouse operations (like packing or stocktaking). 

**Requirements:**

* **Training Time:** A normal warehouse operator with basic computer skills must be able to master core features (Inbound, Outbound, Packing) within 30 minutes of training**.**  
* **Keyboard Shortcuts:** The system must support keyboard shortcuts for fast-paced screens (e.g., press Enter to confirm a packed order, F2 to focus on the barcode scanning box) so operators do not have to use the mouse constantly.   
* **Design Standards:** The user interface layout and color scheme must conform to popular modern design systems (such as Google's Material Design) to create familiarity for users.  
* SKUs and handle up to 20,000 orders generated per day without any performance degradation. 

#### **4.2.2 Performance**

**Description:** The system must process heavy data synchronization with multiple E-commerce platforms swiftly and remain highly responsive under peak sale days (e.g., 11.11, 12.12 Mega Sales). 

**Requirements:** 

* Response Time:  
- Standard user actions (viewing inventory, searching products, filtering orders) must have an average response time of less than 1.5 seconds, and a maximum of 3 seconds under heavy load.  
- API integration responses (such as manually pushing stock updates to Shopee/Lazada) must be completed within 2 seconds.  
* **Throughput:** The backend server must be capable of handling at least 200 concurrent requests per second (RPS) to ensure stability when multiple warehouse staff and automated webhooks from stores are sync-ing data simultaneously.   
* **Data Synchronization Latency:** When a product is sold on Shopee/Lazada, the system must process the stock deduction and synchronize the new inventory level to all other connected stores within 1 minute.   
* **Capacity:** The system database must comfortably accommodate up to 50,000 products.

## **5\. Requirement Appendix** {#5.-requirement-appendix}

### **5.1 Business Rules** {#5.1-business-rules}

| ID | Rule Definition |
| ----- | ----- |
| BR-01 | **Inventory Synchronization:** When an item's stock changes (due to new stock-in, manual edit, or a sale on one platform), the system must immediately recalculate and update the remaining stock to all other connected e-commerce platforms (Shopee, Lazada, TikTok Shop) within 60 seconds.  |
| BR-02 | **Buffer Stock:** Users can set a "buffer stock" (safety stock) value for specific products. The system will automatically report the stock as 0 to e-commerce platforms when the actual stock hits this buffer value to prevent overselling.  |
| BR-03 | **Order Mapping:** Every incoming order from e-commerce platforms must be automatically mapped to a specific registered customer profile and corresponding product SKU in the local database. If no matching SKU is found, the order status must be flagged as "Pending \- Unmapped SKU".  |
| BR-04 | **Inventory Reservation (Holding Status):** When a new order is received, the corresponding product quantity must not be deducted directly from the physical inventory until the items physically leave the warehouse. Instead, the system must move that quantity into a **"Holding"** (Reserved) status. The physical stock is only officially deducted when the order status changes to "Shipped" or "Dispatched".  |
| BR-05 | **Order Cancellation & Stock Return:** If an e-commerce order is cancelled by the buyer or the platform prior to shipping, the system must automatically add the corresponding item quantity back to the local warehouse inventory and update it across all online channels.  |
| BR-06 | **Low-Stock Alert:** The system must trigger an automatic email or dashboard notification to the Warehouse Manager whenever a product's inventory falls below the minimum stock threshold specified in the system setup.  |
| BR-07 | **Inbound Validation:** A warehouse operator cannot complete an "Inbound" (Stock-in) receipt without scanning or entering a valid Purchase Order (PO) ID or Supplier ID for accountability.  |
| BR-08 | **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC):** Only users with the "Warehouse Manager" or "Admin" role can perform inventory adjustments (manual stock modifications or stock writes-off for damaged goods). Regular "Packers" can only view and scan to update order statuses.  |
| BR-09 | **Failed Delivery (Return Goods):** For orders returned by shipping carriers (failed delivery/Boom hàng), the warehouse operator must scan the return waybill. The system will only re-add items to the "Available Stock" after the operator confirms the goods are still in undamaged condition. |
| BR-10 | **SKU Uniqueness:** Each product variant stored in the database must have a completely unique Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) string. The system must reject the creation of any new product that duplicates an existing SKU.  |
| BR-11 | **Multi-Warehouse Allocation:** (If applicable) If the system manages multiple physical warehouses, an e-commerce order must be allocated to the warehouse closest to the customer's shipping address that has sufficient stock available.  |

### **5.2 System Messages** {#5.2-system-messages}

| \# | Message code | Message Type | Context | Content |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | MSG01 | Toast message  | User successfully logs into the system.  | *Login successful. Welcome back, {username}\!*  |
| 2 | MSG02 | In red, under the text box  | A required field (e.g., SKU, Product Name) is left empty upon form submission.  | *This field is required.*   |
| 3 | MSG03 | Toast message (Error)  | The scanned barcode or SKU does not exist in the database.  | *Error: SKU or Barcode "{barcode}" not found in the system.*  |
| 4 | MSG04 | Toast message (Success)  | A warehouse operator successfully scans and packs an order.  | *Order \#{order\_id} has been packed successfully.*  |
| 5 | MSG05 | Toast message (Success)  | Inventory levels are successfully updated and synced to an e-commerce platform.  | *Inventory sync completed successfully for {platform\_name}.*  |
| 6 | MSG06 | Notification Banner (Warning)  | The API connection token with an e-commerce platform has expired or disconnected.  | *Warning: Connection token with {platform\_name} has expired. Please re-authorize.*  |
| 7 | MSG07 | Inline message  | The physical stock of a product falls below its predefined safety buffer limit.  | *Alert: Low stock\! Current physical stock is below the buffer limit.*  |
| 8 | MSG08 | Dialog Pop-up (Error)  | An e-commerce order is fetched but contains a product variant that is not mapped to any local SKU.  | *Failed to process order \#{order\_id}: Contains unmapped SKU.*  |
| 9 | MSG09 | Toast message (Info)  | The system automatically moves a newly ordered item's quantity into the "Holding" status.  | *Quantity {quantity} of SKU {sku} has been moved to Holding status.*  |
| 10 | MSG10 | Dialog Pop-up (Confirm)  | The user clicks the button to disconnect an active e-commerce store integration.  | Are you sure you want to disconnect this {platform\_name} store? This action cannot be undone.  |
